tu.prop: SMS are created through hydrothermal convection and fluid circulation.

tuv.seg: The ocean seafloor and subsurface host a tremendous reservoir for seafloor massive sulfides (SMS).
Le plancher océanique et la subsurface abritent un énorme réservoir de sulfures massifs des fonds marins (SMS).

tu.prop: The ocean seafloor and subsurface host a tremendous reservoir for seafloor massive sulfides (SMS).
They commonly have a high base metal and sulfide content.

tuv.seg: SMS are created through hydrothermal convection and fluid circulation.
Grâce à la convection hydrothermale et à la circulation de fluide hydrothermal, on génère les SMS.

tu.prop: SMS are created through hydrothermal convection and fluid circulation.
Particularly active fluid discharges from hydrothermal chimneys are striking structures on the seafloor.

tuv.seg: They commonly have a high base metal and sulfide content.
Ils ont généralement une teneur élevée en métaux et en sulfures de base.

tu.prop: They commonly have a high base metal and sulfide content.

Along the thermal and chemical gradients in SMS environments a phylogenetically diverse suite of piezophilic to thermophilic and aerobic to anaerobic microorganisms, with versatile metabolic strategies exist: metabolisms include the ability to use numerous inorganic energy sources (e.g., massive sulfides, ferrous iron, H2S, H2), and to fix CO2 autotrophically1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8.

tuv.seg: Particularly active fluid discharges from hydrothermal chimneys are striking structures on the seafloor.
Les décharges de fluides particulièrement actives des cheminées hydrothermales sont des structures impressionnantes du plancher océanique.

tu.prop: Particularly active fluid discharges from hydrothermal chimneys are striking structures on the seafloor.
After hydrothermal activity ceases, the chimney structures continue to react with the ambient low temperature seawater.

tuv.seg: Along the thermal and chemical gradients in SMS environments a phylogenetically diverse suite of piezophilic to thermophilic and aerobic to anaerobic microorganisms, with versatile metabolic strategies exist: metabolisms include the ability to use numerous inorganic energy sources (e.g., massive sulfides, ferrous iron, H2S, H2), and to fix CO2 autotrophically1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8.

Parmi les gradients thermiques et chimiques des environnements SMS, il existe un ensemble phylogénétiquement diversifié de micro-organismes piézophiles à thermophiles et aérobies à anaérobies, avec des stratégies métaboliques variées : métabolismes capables d'utiliser de nombreuses sources d'énergie inorganiques (p. ex. sulfures massifs, fer ferreux, H2S, H2) et de fixer le CO2 de manière autotrophe [1],[2],[3],[4],[5],[6],[7],[8].

tu.prop: Along the thermal and chemical gradients in SMS environments a phylogenetically diverse suite of piezophilic to thermophilic and aerobic to anaerobic microorganisms, with versatile metabolic strategies exist: metabolisms include the ability to use numerous inorganic energy sources (e.g., massive sulfides, ferrous iron, H2S, H2), and to fix CO2 autotrophically1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8.

Inactive hydrothermal chimneys also provide a special habitat where oxidation of metal sulfides marks a significant source for chemosynthetic microorganisms9,10 and appear to be driven to a certain extent by mineralogy11.

tuv.seg: After hydrothermal activity ceases, the chimney structures continue to react with the ambient low temperature seawater.
Lorsque l'activité hydrothermale cesse, les structures des cheminées continuent à réagir avec l'eau de mer ambiante à basse température.

tu.prop: After hydrothermal activity ceases, the chimney structures continue to react with the ambient low temperature seawater.

Given that the chimney habitat itself selects for the community composition but microbial processes in turn also contribute to the formation and conversion of the chimney mineralogical composition, a strong tie between microbe-mineral interactions and biogeochemical cycling is obvious.

tuv.seg: Inactive hydrothermal chimneys also provide a special habitat where oxidation of metal sulfides marks a significant source for chemosynthetic microorganisms9,10 and appear to be driven to a certain extent by mineralogy11.

Les cheminées hydrothermales inactives sont influencées, dans une certaine mesure, par leur composition minéralogique [11]. Elles constituent un habitat particulier ou l'oxydation de sulfures métalliques est une source importante pour les micro-organismes chimio-synthétiques[9],[10].

tu.prop: Inactive hydrothermal chimneys also provide a special habitat where oxidation of metal sulfides marks a significant source for chemosynthetic microorganisms9,10 and appear to be driven to a certain extent by mineralogy11.

In the near future mining of SMS may become economically feasible since mining techniques and extraction technology have advanced and commodity prices and metal demand have increased – due to the accelerating industrialization of countries like China and India12.

tuv.seg: Given that the chimney habitat itself selects for the community composition but microbial processes in turn also contribute to the formation and conversion of the chimney mineralogical composition, a strong tie between microbe-mineral interactions and biogeochemical cycling is obvious.

Les environnements des cheminées déterminent la composition des communautés, or les processus microbiens contribuent à leur tour à la formation et à la conversion de la composition minéralogique des cheminées, il est évident qu'il existe un lien étroit entre les interactions microbes-minéraux et les cycles biogéochimiques.

tu.prop: Given that the chimney habitat itself selects for the community composition but microbial processes in turn also contribute to the formation and conversion of the chimney mineralogical composition, a strong tie between microbe-mineral interactions and biogeochemical cycling is obvious.
SMS potentially worth mining cover extensive areas in and mostly under the seafloor surface and thus can be difficult to find and sample.

tuv.seg: In the near future mining of SMS may become economically feasible since mining techniques and extraction technology have advanced and commodity prices and metal demand have increased – due to the accelerating industrialization of countries like China and India12.

Le développement des techniques et technologies d'extraction, l'augmentation des prix des matières premières et de la demande de métaux, ainsi que l'accélération de l'industrialisation des pays comme la Chine et l'Inde[12], pourraient dans un avenir proche rendre l'exploitation des SMS économiquement intéressante.

tu.prop: In the near future mining of SMS may become economically feasible since mining techniques and extraction technology have advanced and commodity prices and metal demand have increased – due to the accelerating industrialization of countries like China and India12.
In contrast, hydrothermally derived chimney structures are relatively easily accessible sulfide deposits which represent cross sections of SMS habitats found under the seafloor12,13.

tuv.seg: SMS potentially worth mining cover extensive areas in and mostly under the seafloor surface and thus can be difficult to find and sample.

Les SMS présentant un potentiel d'exploitation minière couvrent de vastes zones, pour la plupart situées sous la surface du plancher océanique, et peuvent donc être difficiles à localiser et à analyser.

tu.prop: SMS potentially worth mining cover extensive areas in and mostly under the seafloor surface and thus can be difficult to find and sample.
Therefore, chimneys can be considered as SMS analogues and used to study the impact SMS mining has on microbial diversity and the biogeochemical cycling in the entire area.

tuv.seg: In contrast, hydrothermally derived chimney structures are relatively easily accessible sulfide deposits which represent cross sections of SMS habitats found under the seafloor12,13.

À l'inverse, les structures de cheminées issues de l'activité hydrothermale sont des dépôts de sulfures relativement faciles d'accès, telles des coupes transversales d'habitats de SMS trouvés sous le plancher océanique[12],[13].

tu.prop: In contrast, hydrothermally derived chimney structures are relatively easily accessible sulfide deposits which represent cross sections of SMS habitats found under the seafloor12,13.

From the technical point of view, it is hard to develop mining equipment suitable for low pH and high temperature as encountered in hydrothermally active regions, thus hydrothermally extinct areas are considered for mining.

tuv.seg: Therefore, chimneys can be considered as SMS analogues and used to study the impact SMS mining has on microbial diversity and the biogeochemical cycling in the entire area.

Par conséquent, les cheminées peuvent être considérées comme des analogues de SMS et utilisées pour étudier l'impact de l'exploitation des SMS sur la diversité microbienne et le cycle biogéochimique dans l'ensemble de la zone.

tu.prop: Therefore, chimneys can be considered as SMS analogues and used to study the impact SMS mining has on microbial diversity and the biogeochemical cycling in the entire area.

However, while active chimneys can be regenerated in weeks to years, SMS in hydrothermally extinct fields take tens of thousands of years to concentrate economically interesting metals in large amounts, indicating that the deposits are not renewable resources14.

tuv.seg: From the technical point of view, it is hard to develop mining equipment suitable for low pH and high temperature as encountered in hydrothermally active regions, thus hydrothermally extinct areas are considered for mining.

Pour des raisons techniques, il est difficile de concevoir des équipements miniers adaptés à un pH faible et à des températures élevées, comme c'est le cas dans les régions hydrothermales actives. C'est pourquoi, l'exploitation minière est envisagée dans les zones inactives.

tu.prop: From the technical point of view, it is hard to develop mining equipment suitable for low pH and high temperature as encountered in hydrothermally active regions, thus hydrothermally extinct areas are considered for mining.
Many environmental impacts of deep-sea mining have been predicted, such as changes in water quality, sedimentation, endemic fauna and microbial communities12,15,16,17,18.

tuv.seg: However, while active chimneys can be regenerated in weeks to years, SMS in hydrothermally extinct fields take tens of thousands of years to concentrate economically interesting metals in large amounts, indicating that the deposits are not renewable resources14.

Toutefois, alors que les cheminées actives peuvent être régénérées en quelques semaines ou quelques années, il faut des dizaines de milliers d'années pour que les SMS des zones inactives produisent des quantités importantes de métaux intéressants d'un point de vue économique, ce qui indique que les dépôts ne sont pas des ressources renouvelables[14].

tu.prop: However, while active chimneys can be regenerated in weeks to years, SMS in hydrothermally extinct fields take tens of thousands of years to concentrate economically interesting metals in large amounts, indicating that the deposits are not renewable resources14.
SMS mining activities will produce tons of drifting particles, which might clog and contaminate nearby environments and interfere with environmental processes19.

tuv.seg: Many environmental impacts of deep-sea mining have been predicted, such as changes in water quality, sedimentation, endemic fauna and microbial communities12,15,16,17,18.

De nombreux impacts environnementaux de l’exploitation minière des fonds sous-marins ont été quantifiés, tels que : des modifications de la qualité de l'eau, de la sédimentation, de la faune endémique mais aussi des communautés microbiennes [12],[15],[16],[17],[18].

tu.prop: Many environmental impacts of deep-sea mining have been predicted, such as changes in water quality, sedimentation, endemic fauna and microbial communities12,15,16,17,18.
Also, mining activities cause mixing of solid with liquid components on the sea floor and change essential nutrients and water properties in the deep-sea19.

tuv.seg: SMS mining activities will produce tons of drifting particles, which might clog and contaminate nearby environments and interfere with environmental processes19.
Le minage des SMS sera responsable du déplacement de tonnes de particules, ce qui pourrait encrasser et contaminer les environnements proches et interférer avec leur fonctionnement[19].

tu.prop: SMS mining activities will produce tons of drifting particles, which might clog and contaminate nearby environments and interfere with environmental processes19.

With only a few of the possible effects pointed out here, deep-sea mining of SMS deposits may result in the loss of ecologically important habitats, change the local benthic community and destroy the region’s ability to sustain itself.

tuv.seg: Also, mining activities cause mixing of solid with liquid components on the sea floor and change essential nutrients and water properties in the deep-sea19.

En outre, les activités minières engendrent un mélange entre des composants solides et liquides au niveau du plancher océanique et entraînent une modification de nutriments essentiels et de certaines propriétés de l'eau des fonds marins[19].

tu.prop: Also, mining activities cause mixing of solid with liquid components on the sea floor and change essential nutrients and water properties in the deep-sea19.
To minimize the risk of environmental destruction and to maintain the sustainability of marine environments, it is very important to design suitable management strategies for deep-sea mining.

tuv.seg: With only a few of the possible effects pointed out here, deep-sea mining of SMS deposits may result in the loss of ecologically important habitats, change the local benthic community and destroy the region’s ability to sustain itself.

Avec seulement, quelques-uns des effets possibles mentionnés ici, l'exploitation des dépôts de SMS dans les fonds sous-marins pourrait entraîner une perte d'habitats biologiquement importants, modifier la communauté benthique locale et détruire la capacité d'autosuffisance de la zone.

tu.prop: With only a few of the possible effects pointed out here, deep-sea mining of SMS deposits may result in the loss of ecologically important habitats, change the local benthic community and destroy the region’s ability to sustain itself.

In 2015 the International Seabed Authority (ISA) assigned a license to the German Federal Institute for Geosciences and Natural Resources (BGR) to explore deep-sea hydrothermal vents in a specific area of the southwestern Indian Ocean, which is enriched in massive polymetallic sulfides.

tuv.seg: To minimize the risk of environmental destruction and to maintain the sustainability of marine environments, it is very important to design suitable management strategies for deep-sea mining.

Afin de minimiser le risque de destruction environnementale et de préserver la durabilité des environnements marins, il est essentiel de mettre en place des stratégies de gestion adaptées à l'exploitation minière sous-marine.

tu.prop: To minimize the risk of environmental destruction and to maintain the sustainability of marine environments, it is very important to design suitable management strategies for deep-sea mining.
The work presented here includes sites located in the Kairei field and the newly found Pelagia vent field.

tuv.seg: In 2015 the International Seabed Authority (ISA) assigned a license to the German Federal Institute for Geosciences and Natural Resources (BGR) to explore deep-sea hydrothermal vents in a specific area of the southwestern Indian Ocean, which is enriched in massive polymetallic sulfides.

En 2015, l’Autorité internationale des fonds marins (ISA) a attribué une licence d'exploration des cheminées hydrothermales sous-marines dans une zone particulière au sud-ouest de l’océan Indien à l'Institut fédéral des géosciences et de ressources naturelles allemand (BGR). Cette zone est riche en sulfures polymétalliques.

tu.prop: In 2015 the International Seabed Authority (ISA) assigned a license to the German Federal Institute for Geosciences and Natural Resources (BGR) to explore deep-sea hydrothermal vents in a specific area of the southwestern Indian Ocean, which is enriched in massive polymetallic sulfides.
The Kairei hydrothermal field was first discovered in 2001 along the Central Indian Ridge (CIR)20,21.

tuv.seg: The work presented here includes sites located in the Kairei field and the newly found Pelagia vent field.
Le travail présenté ci-dessous mentionne des zones situées dans le champ hydrothermal de Kairei et dans le récemment découvert champ de Pelagia.

tu.prop: The work presented here includes sites located in the Kairei field and the newly found Pelagia vent field.
Since then, a geochemical and geological20,22,23 as well as a biological9,21,24,25 background has been established.

tuv.seg: The Kairei hydrothermal field was first discovered in 2001 along the Central Indian Ridge (CIR)20,21.
Le champ hydrothermal de Kairei a été découvert en 2001, le long de la Dorsale centrale indienne (CIR)[20],[21].

tu.prop: The Kairei hydrothermal field was first discovered in 2001 along the Central Indian Ridge (CIR)20,21.
The Kairei chimneys contain massive sulfide precipitates22,26 and mafic to ultramafic wall rocks are discussed to represent the metal source.

tuv.seg: Since then, a geochemical and geological20,22,23 as well as a biological9,21,24,25 background has been established.
Depuis, un environnement géochimique, géologique[20],[22],[23], mais aussi biologique[9],[21],[24],[25], y a été découvert.

tu.prop: Since then, a geochemical and geological20,22,23 as well as a biological9,21,24,25 background has been established.

The major mineral components of inactive chimney structures from Kairei are chalcopyrite (CuFeS2) and pyrrhotite (Fe1−xS)9, which previously were precipitated from metal-rich hydrothermal vent fluids26.

tuv.seg: The Kairei chimneys contain massive sulfide precipitates22,26 and mafic to ultramafic wall rocks are discussed to represent the metal source.
Les cheminées de Kairei contiennent des dépôts de sulfures massifs[22],[26] et des murs de roches mafiques et ultramafiques sont soupçonnées d’être la source de métal.

tu.prop: The Kairei chimneys contain massive sulfide precipitates22,26 and mafic to ultramafic wall rocks are discussed to represent the metal source.
The emitted fluids have a low pH and are enriched in H2S and H2, but hold low methane concentrations27.

tuv.seg: The major mineral components of inactive chimney structures from Kairei are chalcopyrite (CuFeS2) and pyrrhotite (Fe1−xS)9, which previously were precipitated from metal-rich hydrothermal vent fluids26.

Les principaux composants minéraux des cheminées inactives de Kairei sont la chalcopyrite (CuFeS2) et la pyrrhotite (Fe1−xS)[9], auparavant précipités à partir des fluides hydrothermaux riches en métaux[26].

tu.prop: The major mineral components of inactive chimney structures from Kairei are chalcopyrite (CuFeS2) and pyrrhotite (Fe1−xS)9, which previously were precipitated from metal-rich hydrothermal vent fluids26.
Some studies on the microbial communities from inactive chimneys9,11 as well as hydrothermal fluids and active chimneys24,25 already exist for the Kairei vent field.

tuv.seg: The emitted fluids have a low pH and are enriched in H2S and H2, but hold low methane concentrations27.
Ces fluides ont un faible pH et sont enrichis en H2S et H2 cependant, ils contiennent de faibles concentrations en méthane[27].

tu.prop: The emitted fluids have a low pH and are enriched in H2S and H2, but hold low methane concentrations27.

Their microbial communities have also been compared to those from other chimney habitats, demonstrating diverse microbial communities in active chimneys and show that mineralogy is an important factor for shaping inactive chimney microbial community compositions11,25.

tuv.seg: Some studies on the microbial communities from inactive chimneys9,11 as well as hydrothermal fluids and active chimneys24,25 already exist for the Kairei vent field.

Certaines études sur les communautés microbiennes des cheminées inactives[9],[11] mais aussi sur les fluides hydrothermaux et les cheminées actives[24],[25] existent déjà pour le champ hydrothermal de Kairei.

tu.prop: Some studies on the microbial communities from inactive chimneys9,11 as well as hydrothermal fluids and active chimneys24,25 already exist for the Kairei vent field.
However, differences and similarities between communities from Kairei active and inactive chimneys were not assessed.

tuv.seg: Their microbial communities have also been compared to those from other chimney habitats, demonstrating diverse microbial communities in active chimneys and show that mineralogy is an important factor for shaping inactive chimney microbial community compositions11,25.

Leurs communautés microbiennes ont également été comparées à celles d’autres cheminées, ce qui a mis en lumière leur diversité au niveau des cheminées actives et montré que la minéralogie est un facteur important dans la structuration de la composition des communautés microbiennes des cheminées inactives[11],[25].

tu.prop: Their microbial communities have also been compared to those from other chimney habitats, demonstrating diverse microbial communities in active chimneys and show that mineralogy is an important factor for shaping inactive chimney microbial community compositions11,25.

While the Kairei area was already studied for more than a decade, the Pelagia vent field at the Southeast Indian Ridge was newly discovered during the cruise INDEX2014 (BGR, pers. comm.) and this is the first report on its microbial and mineralogical composition.

tuv.seg: However, differences and similarities between communities from Kairei active and inactive chimneys were not assessed.
Toutefois, les différences et similitudes entre les communautés des cheminées actives et inactives de Kairei n'ont pas été examinées.

tu.prop: However, differences and similarities between communities from Kairei active and inactive chimneys were not assessed.
The aim of this study was to identify whether active venting and inactive chimney structures in the Kairei and Pelagia areas host habitat specific bacterial and archaeal communities.

tuv.seg: While the Kairei area was already studied for more than a decade, the Pelagia vent field at the Southeast Indian Ridge was newly discovered during the cruise INDEX2014 (BGR, pers. comm.) and this is the first report on its microbial and mineralogical composition.

Le site de Kairei a déjà été étudié pendant plus de dix ans, mais le champ hydrothermal de Pelagia de la dorsale sud-est-indienne a été découvert pendant la campagne INDEX2014 (BGR, communication personnelle). Ceci est le premier rapport sur sa composition microbienne et minéralogique.

tu.prop: While the Kairei area was already studied for more than a decade, the Pelagia vent field at the Southeast Indian Ridge was newly discovered during the cruise INDEX2014 (BGR, pers. comm.) and this is the first report on its microbial and mineralogical composition.

Since hydrothermally formed chimneys can be considered as windows into SMS areas, targeted for mining, the knowledge of the specificity of chimney communities can give insights into the possible “diversity loss”, if removed through mining from the seafloor.

tuv.seg: The aim of this study was to identify whether active venting and inactive chimney structures in the Kairei and Pelagia areas host habitat specific bacterial and archaeal communities.

L'objectif de cette étude était de déterminer si les structures formées par les cheminées hydrothermales actives et inactives des sites de Kairei et Pelagia hébergent des communautés de bactériennes et archéennes spécifiques à ces habitats.

tu.prop: The aim of this study was to identify whether active venting and inactive chimney structures in the Kairei and Pelagia areas host habitat specific bacterial and archaeal communities.
This can help in assessing the role of altered biogeochemical cycling for the benthic habitat.

tuv.seg: Since hydrothermally formed chimneys can be considered as windows into SMS areas, targeted for mining, the knowledge of the specificity of chimney communities can give insights into the possible “diversity loss”, if removed through mining from the seafloor.

Les cheminées hydrothermales peuvent être considérées comme analogues aux zones de SMS visées par l'exploitation minière. Il est donc utile de connaître la spécificité des communautés vivant dans les cheminées pour avoir un aperçu de la possible "perte de diversité" causée par l'exploitation des fonds marins.

tu.prop: Since hydrothermally formed chimneys can be considered as windows into SMS areas, targeted for mining, the knowledge of the specificity of chimney communities can give insights into the possible “diversity loss”, if removed through mining from the seafloor.

tuv.seg: This can help in assessing the role of altered biogeochemical cycling for the benthic habitat.
Cela peut aider à évaluer le rôle joué par le cycle biogéochimique altéré dans l'habitat benthique.

tu.prop: This can help in assessing the role of altered biogeochemical cycling for the benthic habitat.

The aim of this study was to identify taxa that appear to be highly specific for chimney structures as analogues for SMS to assess the potential diversity loss if mining of comparable structures would take place.

tuv.seg: Results and Discussion
Résultats et discussions

tu.prop: For this purpose we assessed the microbial communities of fourteen rock, fluid and water samples from the Kairei and Pelagia venting areas and nearby in the Indian Ocean.

tuv.seg: The aim of this study was to identify taxa that appear to be highly specific for chimney structures as analogues for SMS to assess the potential diversity loss if mining of comparable structures would take place.

L'objectif de cette étude était d'identifier les taxons qui semblent être très spécifiques aux structures formées par les cheminées et considérées comme analogues aux SMS, pour évaluer la perte de diversité qui pourrait être causée par l'exploitation minière de structures comparables.

tu.prop: The aim of this study was to identify taxa that appear to be highly specific for chimney structures as analogues for SMS to assess the potential diversity loss if mining of comparable structures would take place.
Mineralogy of the chimney structures was described and linked to the presence of taxa.

tuv.seg: For this purpose we assessed the microbial communities of fourteen rock, fluid and water samples from the Kairei and Pelagia venting areas and nearby in the Indian Ocean.

Dans ce but, nous avons étudié les communautés microbiennes de quatorze échantillons de roches, de fluides et d'eau prélévés dans les zones de cheminées de Kairei et Pelagia et des zones proches dans l'Océan Indien.

tu.prop: For this purpose we assessed the microbial communities of fourteen rock, fluid and water samples from the Kairei and Pelagia venting areas and nearby in the Indian Ocean.
OTUs (Operational Taxonomic Units) specific to a habitat type were identified.

tuv.seg: Mineralogy of the chimney structures was described and linked to the presence of taxa.
La minéralogie des structures formées par les cheminées a été décrite et reliée à la présence de taxons.

tu.prop: Mineralogy of the chimney structures was described and linked to the presence of taxa.

Using beta diversity analyses we investigated the similarities and dissimilarities among the samples in the Kairei and Pelagia environments and illustrated their relation to samples from comparable biotopes available from the literature.

tuv.seg: OTUs (Operational Taxonomic Units) specific to a habitat type were identified.
Des OTU spécifiques à un type d'habitat ont été identifiées.

tu.prop: OTUs (Operational Taxonomic Units) specific to a habitat type were identified.
Mineralogical and geochemical relations of hydrothermal precipitates

tuv.seg: Using beta diversity analyses we investigated the similarities and dissimilarities among the samples in the Kairei and Pelagia environments and illustrated their relation to samples from comparable biotopes available from the literature.

A l'aide d'analyses de diversité bêta, nous avons étudié les similarités et les différences entre les échantillons des environnements de Kairei et Pelagia et illustré leur relation avec des échantillons de biotopes comparables provenant de la littérature.

tu.prop: Using beta diversity analyses we investigated the similarities and dissimilarities among the samples in the Kairei and Pelagia environments and illustrated their relation to samples from comparable biotopes available from the literature.

From the sulfide precipitates collected during the INDEX2016 cruise seven active and inactive chimney samples were selected as containing two types of mineralization: i) three copper-rich chimney edifices and fragments and ii) four beehive-shaped iron-zinc-rich chimneys (see Table 1).

tuv.seg: Mineralogical and geochemical relations of hydrothermal precipitates
Relations minéralogiques et géochimiques des précipités hydrothermaux

tu.prop: Mineralogical and geochemical relations of hydrothermal precipitates
Copper-rich chimney edifices and fragments (S1, S7, S8) sampled from the Kairei hydrothermal field are dominated by chalcopyrite, with minor pyrite, marcasite, wurtzite and sphalerite.

tuv.seg: From the sulfide precipitates collected during the INDEX2016 cruise seven active and inactive chimney samples were selected as containing two types of mineralization: i) three copper-rich chimney edifices and fragments and ii) four beehive-shaped iron-zinc-rich chimneys (see Table 1).

Parmi les précipités sulfurés prélevés pendant la campagne INDEX2016, sept échantillons de cheminées actives et inactives ont été sélectionnés car ils contenaient deux types de minéralisation : i) trois échantillons de fragments et édifices de cheminées riches en cuivre et ii) quatre échantillons de cheminées en forme de nids d'abeille et riches en fer et zinc (voir Tableau 1).

tu.prop: From the sulfide precipitates collected during the INDEX2016 cruise seven active and inactive chimney samples were selected as containing two types of mineralization: i) three copper-rich chimney edifices and fragments and ii) four beehive-shaped iron-zinc-rich chimneys (see Table 1).
Conduits are lined by massive chalcopyrite coexisting with granular wurtzite and pyrite/marcasite.

tuv.seg: Copper-rich chimney edifices and fragments (S1, S7, S8) sampled from the Kairei hydrothermal field are dominated by chalcopyrite, with minor pyrite, marcasite, wurtzite and sphalerite.

Les édifices et fragments de cheminées riches en cuivre (E1, E7, E8) prélevés dans le champ hydrothermal de Kairei sont majoritairement composés de chalcopyrite, avec une quantité moindre de pyrite, de marcassite, de wurtzite et de sphalérite.

tu.prop: Copper-rich chimney edifices and fragments (S1, S7, S8) sampled from the Kairei hydrothermal field are dominated by chalcopyrite, with minor pyrite, marcasite, wurtzite and sphalerite.

Wurtzite grains often occur within the massive chalcopyrite aggregates, with zonal enrichment of secondary chalcopyrite inclusions attesting to replacement processes forming so-called chalcopyrite diseases textures28.

tuv.seg: Conduits are lined by massive chalcopyrite coexisting with granular wurtzite and pyrite/marcasite.
Les conduits sont composés de chalcopyrite massive coexistant avec de la wurtzite granulaire et de la pyrite/marcassite.

tu.prop: Conduits are lined by massive chalcopyrite coexisting with granular wurtzite and pyrite/marcasite.
The occurrence and grain size of granular chalcopyrite decrease towards the outer parts of the chimneys.

tuv.seg: Wurtzite grains often occur within the massive chalcopyrite aggregates, with zonal enrichment of secondary chalcopyrite inclusions attesting to replacement processes forming so-called chalcopyrite diseases textures28.

Souvent, les grains de wurtzite se forment au sein d'agrégats de chalcopyrite massive. L'enrichissement des inclusions secondaires de chalcopyrite dans cette zone atteste des processus de remplacement formant ces textures de "maladie de la chalcopyrite"[28].

tu.prop: Wurtzite grains often occur within the massive chalcopyrite aggregates, with zonal enrichment of secondary chalcopyrite inclusions attesting to replacement processes forming so-called chalcopyrite diseases textures28.
Simultaneously an increasing amount of disseminated sphalerite coexisting with pyrite and minor chalcopyrite can be observed in the outer parts.

tuv.seg: The occurrence and grain size of granular chalcopyrite decrease towards the outer parts of the chimneys.
L'occurrence et la taille des grains de chalcopyrite granulaire diminuent vers les parties externes des cheminées.

tu.prop: The occurrence and grain size of granular chalcopyrite decrease towards the outer parts of the chimneys.
This indicates decreasing temperatures towards the outer parts of the chimney due to changes in the precipitation of chalcopyrite and sphalerite.

tuv.seg: Simultaneously an increasing amount of disseminated sphalerite coexisting with pyrite and minor chalcopyrite can be observed in the outer parts.
Simultanément, une quantité croissante de sphalérite disséminée coexiste avec de la pyrite et de la chalcopyrite mineure, elle peut également être observée dans ces parties externes.

tu.prop: Simultaneously an increasing amount of disseminated sphalerite coexisting with pyrite and minor chalcopyrite can be observed in the outer parts.
Active chimney edifices and fragments contain accessory, lath-shaped and fibrous relics of anhydrite filling interstices and occasionally inactive conduits.

tuv.seg: This indicates decreasing temperatures towards the outer parts of the chimney due to changes in the precipitation of chalcopyrite and sphalerite.
Cela indique une diminution des températures au niveau des parties externes de la cheminée en raison de changements dans la précipitation de chalcopyrite et de sphalérite.

tu.prop: This indicates decreasing temperatures towards the outer parts of the chimney due to changes in the precipitation of chalcopyrite and sphalerite.
The absence of anhydrite in inactive chimney edifices (S1) attests to anhydrite solubility in seawater during cooling.

tuv.seg: Active chimney edifices and fragments contain accessory, lath-shaped and fibrous relics of anhydrite filling interstices and occasionally inactive conduits.

Les édifices et fragments de cheminées actives contiennent des reliques accessoires en lamelles fibreuses composées d'anhydrite, qui remplissent les interstices et, occasionnellement, les conduits inactifs.

tu.prop: Active chimney edifices and fragments contain accessory, lath-shaped and fibrous relics of anhydrite filling interstices and occasionally inactive conduits.
All chimney walls are coated with iron-oxyhydroxide due to seawater oxidation.

tuv.seg: The absence of anhydrite in inactive chimney edifices (S1) attests to anhydrite solubility in seawater during cooling.
L'absence d'anhydrite dans les édifices de cheminée inactives (E1) témoigne de la solubilité de l'anhydrite dans l'eau de mer pendant le refroidissement.

tu.prop: The absence of anhydrite in inactive chimney edifices (S1) attests to anhydrite solubility in seawater during cooling.
Iron-zinc-rich beehive chimneys were sampled at the Kairei (S2) and the Pelagia (S4, S5, S6) hydrothermal field.

tuv.seg: All chimney walls are coated with iron-oxyhydroxide due to seawater oxidation.
Toutes les parois des cheminées sont recouvertes d'oxyhydroxyde de fer à cause de l'oxydation par l'eau de mer.

tu.prop: All chimney walls are coated with iron-oxyhydroxide due to seawater oxidation.
They contain complex intergrowth of different minerals and mineral textures, with large amounts of homogeneously distributed pore spaces that allow fluid flow towards the chimney walls29.

tuv.seg: Iron-zinc-rich beehive chimneys were sampled at the Kairei (S2) and the Pelagia (S4, S5, S6) hydrothermal field.
Des cheminées en forme de ruche et riches en fer et zinc ont été prélevés dans les champs hydrothermaux de Kairei (E2) et de Pelagia (E4, E5, E6).

tu.prop: Iron-zinc-rich beehive chimneys were sampled at the Kairei (S2) and the Pelagia (S4, S5, S6) hydrothermal field.
The high porosity derives from aggregates of collomorph pyrite/marcasite, sphalerite surrounded by chalcopyrite, lath-shaped pyrrhotite and amorphous silica.

tuv.seg: They contain complex intergrowth of different minerals and mineral textures, with large amounts of homogeneously distributed pore spaces that allow fluid flow towards the chimney walls29.

Elles contiennent une intercroissance complexe de différents minéraux et textures minérales, avec des espaces poreux en grande quantité et répartis de manière homogène, permettant l'écoulement des fluides vers les parois de la cheminée[29].

tu.prop: They contain complex intergrowth of different minerals and mineral textures, with large amounts of homogeneously distributed pore spaces that allow fluid flow towards the chimney walls29.
Chimney interiors are characterized by traces of sulfides formed from high-temperature precipitation such as chalcopyrite-isocubanite surrounded by pyrite and filling conduits.

tuv.seg: The high porosity derives from aggregates of collomorph pyrite/marcasite, sphalerite surrounded by chalcopyrite, lath-shaped pyrrhotite and amorphous silica.
Cette forte porosité provient des agrégats de pyrite/marcassite collomorphe de sphalérite entourée de chalcopyrite, de lamelles de pyrrhotite et de silice amorphe.

tu.prop: The high porosity derives from aggregates of collomorph pyrite/marcasite, sphalerite surrounded by chalcopyrite, lath-shaped pyrrhotite and amorphous silica.
Outer portions of the chimneys consist of lower-temperature precipitates such as sphalerite, pyrite/marcasite and locally amorphous silica.

tuv.seg: Chimney interiors are characterized by traces of sulfides formed from high-temperature precipitation such as chalcopyrite-isocubanite surrounded by pyrite and filling conduits.

L'intérieur des cheminées est caractérisé par des traces de sulfures formés à partir de précipités à températures élevées telles que la chalcopyrite-isocubanite entourée de pyrite et qui remplit les conduits.

tu.prop: Chimney interiors are characterized by traces of sulfides formed from high-temperature precipitation such as chalcopyrite-isocubanite surrounded by pyrite and filling conduits.
Active chimneys are nearly fresh and weathering products can only be observed on the outer wall in contact with seawater.

tuv.seg: Outer portions of the chimneys consist of lower-temperature precipitates such as sphalerite, pyrite/marcasite and locally amorphous silica.
La partie externe des cheminées se composent de précipités à plus basse température tels que la sphalérite, la pyrite/marcassite et localement la silice amorphe.

tu.prop: Outer portions of the chimneys consist of lower-temperature precipitates such as sphalerite, pyrite/marcasite and locally amorphous silica.
However, inactive chimney edifices (S4) show starting weathering processes reflected by replacement of chalcopyrite-isocubanite by secondary copper minerals (e.g. covellite).

tuv.seg: Active chimneys are nearly fresh and weathering products can only be observed on the outer wall in contact with seawater.
Les cheminées actives ne sont presque pas altérées et des produits d'altération ne sont observables que sur la paroi externe en contact avec l'eau de mer.

tu.prop: Active chimneys are nearly fresh and weathering products can only be observed on the outer wall in contact with seawater.
Active as well as inactive chimney edifices, are characterized by distinct compositional zoning from the core towards the rim reflecting strong gradients in fluid temperature and composition30,31.

tuv.seg: However, inactive chimney edifices (S4) show starting weathering processes reflected by replacement of chalcopyrite-isocubanite by secondary copper minerals (e.g. covellite).

Cependant, les édifices de cheminées inactives (E4) montrent des processus d'altération incipiant mis en évidence par le remplacement de la chalcopyrite-isocubanite par des minéraux de cuivre secondaires (par exemple, la covellite).

tu.prop: However, inactive chimney edifices (S4) show starting weathering processes reflected by replacement of chalcopyrite-isocubanite by secondary copper minerals (e.g. covellite).

Mineral and geochemical compositions of copper-rich chimney edifices (S1, S7, S8) indicate formation conditions at temperatures at or above 300 °C. Specific fluid compositions cause higher amounts of high-temperature precipitates than in iron-zinc-rich beehive chimneys (S2, S4, S5, S6).

tuv.seg: Active as well as inactive chimney edifices, are characterized by distinct compositional zoning from the core towards the rim reflecting strong gradients in fluid temperature and composition30,31.

Les édifices de cheminées actives comme inactives se caractérisent par une zonation compositionnelle distincte du noyau vers le bord, reflétant de forts gradients de température et de composition des fluides[30],[31].

tu.prop: Active as well as inactive chimney edifices, are characterized by distinct compositional zoning from the core towards the rim reflecting strong gradients in fluid temperature and composition30,31.
Bacterial and archaeal communities of the Kairei and Pelagia areas

tuv.seg: Mineral and geochemical compositions of copper-rich chimney edifices (S1, S7, S8) indicate formation conditions at temperatures at or above 300 °C. Specific fluid compositions cause higher amounts of high-temperature precipitates than in iron-zinc-rich beehive chimneys (S2, S4, S5, S6).

Les compositions minérales et géochimiques des édifices de cheminées riches en cuivre (E1, E7, E8) indiquent les conditions de formation à des températures égales ou supérieures à 300 °C. Des compositions fluides spécifiques provoquent des quantités plus élevées de précipités à haute température que dans les cheminées en ruche riches en fer et en zinc (E2, E4, E5, E6).

tu.prop: Mineral and geochemical compositions of copper-rich chimney edifices (S1, S7, S8) indicate formation conditions at temperatures at or above 300 °C. Specific fluid compositions cause higher amounts of high-temperature precipitates than in iron-zinc-rich beehive chimneys (S2, S4, S5, S6).

Kairei and Pelagia microbial communities from three active chimneys and four inactive chimneys (for Archaea three inactive chimneys) were compared with two hydrothermal fluids, one sulfide talus sample, two plume water samples and two open ocean samples (Table 2).

tuv.seg: Bacterial and archaeal communities of the Kairei and Pelagia areas
Communautés bactériennes et archéennes des zones de Kairei et Pelagia

tu.prop: Bacterial and archaeal communities of the Kairei and Pelagia areas

Our sequencing yielded 10.3 million bacterial and 9.6 million archaeal 16S rRNA amplicons, which resulted in 140785 and 121333, respectively, OTUs (at a dissimilarity of 3%) (Table 2, and Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: Kairei and Pelagia microbial communities from three active chimneys and four inactive chimneys (for Archaea three inactive chimneys) were compared with two hydrothermal fluids, one sulfide talus sample, two plume water samples and two open ocean samples (Table 2).

Les communautés microbiennes de Kairei et Pelagia provenant de trois cheminées actives et de quatre cheminées inactives (trois cheminées inactives pour les archées) ont été comparées à deux échantillons de fluides hydrothermaux, un échantillon de débris de sulfure, deux échantillons d'eau du panache et deux échantillons de l'océan ouvert (Tableau 2).

tu.prop: Kairei and Pelagia microbial communities from three active chimneys and four inactive chimneys (for Archaea three inactive chimneys) were compared with two hydrothermal fluids, one sulfide talus sample, two plume water samples and two open ocean samples (Table 2).
Total cell counts of the fluids were between 1.3 × 104 ± 1.6 × 103 cells ml−1 and 1.2 × 105 ± 5.7 × 104 cells ml−1 (Table 2).

tuv.seg: Our sequencing yielded 10.3 million bacterial and 9.6 million archaeal 16S rRNA amplicons, which resulted in 140785 and 121333, respectively, OTUs (at a dissimilarity of 3%) (Table 2, and Supplementary Table S1).

Notre séquençage a produit 10,3 millions d'amplicons d'ARNr 16S bactériens et 9,6 millions d'amplicons d'ARNr 16S archéens, ce qui a donné respectivement 140 785 et 121 333 OTU (à une dissimilarité de 3%). (Tableau 2 et tableau supplémentaire S1)

tu.prop: Our sequencing yielded 10.3 million bacterial and 9.6 million archaeal 16S rRNA amplicons, which resulted in 140785 and 121333, respectively, OTUs (at a dissimilarity of 3%) (Table 2, and Supplementary Table S1).
Rarefaction curves of the sequenced samples demonstrate a similar good coverage of both the bacterial and archaeal communities (Supplementary Fig. S1).

tuv.seg: Total cell counts of the fluids were between 1.3 × 104 ± 1.6 × 103 cells ml−1 and 1.2 × 105 ± 5.7 × 104 cells ml−1 (Table 2).
Le nombre total de cellules des fluides était compris entre 1,3 × 104 ± 1,6 × 103 cellules ml-1 et 1,2 × 105 ± 5,7 × 104 cellules ml-1 (Tableau 2).

tu.prop: Total cell counts of the fluids were between 1.3 × 104 ± 1.6 × 103 cells ml−1 and 1.2 × 105 ± 5.7 × 104 cells ml−1 (Table 2).

In our analyses, we applied an open-reference OTU picking procedure32, which first collects sequences into OTUs by similarity to database sequences (here termed reference-based OTUs) and clusters the remaining sequences into OTUs by their similarity to each other (here termed de novo OTUs).

tuv.seg: Rarefaction curves of the sequenced samples demonstrate a similar good coverage of both the bacterial and archaeal communities (Supplementary Fig. S1).
Les courbes de raréfaction des échantillons séquencés montrent une couverture similaire et satisfaisante des communautés bactériennes et archéennes (Données S1 supplémentaire).

tu.prop: Rarefaction curves of the sequenced samples demonstrate a similar good coverage of both the bacterial and archaeal communities (Supplementary Fig. S1).

Most OTUs in both the bacterial (96%) and archaeal (99%) communities were de novo OTUs, although sequence counts were lower than those of reference-based OTUs, i.e. they consist of 31% and 34%, of the total bacterial and archaeal 16S tags, respectively (Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: In our analyses, we applied an open-reference OTU picking procedure32, which first collects sequences into OTUs by similarity to database sequences (here termed reference-based OTUs) and clusters the remaining sequences into OTUs by their similarity to each other (here termed de novo OTUs).

Dans nos analyses, nous avons appliqué une procédure[32] de classification taxonomique en "open-reference". Elle regroupe d'abord les séquences en OTU par similarité avec les séquences de la base de données (ici appelées OTU basées sur des références) et regroupe ensuite les séquences restantes en OTU en fonction de leur similarité entre elles (ici appelées OTU de novo).

tu.prop: In our analyses, we applied an open-reference OTU picking procedure32, which first collects sequences into OTUs by similarity to database sequences (here termed reference-based OTUs) and clusters the remaining sequences into OTUs by their similarity to each other (here termed de novo OTUs).

The results of the taxonomic assignments of the bacterial and archaeal OTUs identified in the different habitats are presented as plots in the supplementary results, summarized as phyla/class levels (Supplementary Fig. S2a-f), orders (Supplementary Fig. S3a-c) and genera (Supplementary Fig. S4a-f).

tuv.seg: Most OTUs in both the bacterial (96%) and archaeal (99%) communities were de novo OTUs, although sequence counts were lower than those of reference-based OTUs, i.e. they consist of 31% and 34%, of the total bacterial and archaeal 16S tags, respectively (Supplementary Table S1).

La plupart des OTU dans les communautés bactériennes (96 %) et archéennes (99 %) étaient des OTU de novo, bien que le nombre de séquences était inférieur à celui des OTU basées sur des références. Il représentait respectivement 31 % et 34 % du nombre total de séquences 16S bactériennes et archéennes.

tu.prop: Most OTUs in both the bacterial (96%) and archaeal (99%) communities were de novo OTUs, although sequence counts were lower than those of reference-based OTUs, i.e. they consist of 31% and 34%, of the total bacterial and archaeal 16S tags, respectively (Supplementary Table S1).

Furthermore, to gain a better understanding of the OTUs that are of relevance for a given habitat we listed those that were found in our analyses exclusively (Supplementary Tables S2, S3 and S4) or were significantly more abundant in one habitat type (Supplementary Tables S5).

tuv.seg: The results of the taxonomic assignments of the bacterial and archaeal OTUs identified in the different habitats are presented as plots in the supplementary results, summarized as phyla/class levels (Supplementary Fig. S2a-f), orders (Supplementary Fig. S3a-c) and genera (Supplementary Fig. S4a-f).

Les résultats des affectations taxonomiques des OTU bactériennes et archéennes identifiées dans les différents habitats sont présentés sous forme de graphiques dans les résultats supplémentaires, résumés en niveaux de phyla/classe (Fig. S2a-f supplémentaire), ordres (Fig. S3a-c supplémentaire) et genres (Données Supplémentaire S4a-f).

tu.prop: The results of the taxonomic assignments of the bacterial and archaeal OTUs identified in the different habitats are presented as plots in the supplementary results, summarized as phyla/class levels (Supplementary Fig. S2a-f), orders (Supplementary Fig. S3a-c) and genera (Supplementary Fig. S4a-f).

As we have shown in a previous study33 apparent habitat endemicity in 16S analyses of the microbial communities for marine environments is in many cases likely to be an artifact of an insufficient coverage so that individuals of very rare OTUs are not detected.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, to gain a better understanding of the OTUs that are of relevance for a given habitat we listed those that were found in our analyses exclusively (Supplementary Tables S2, S3 and S4) or were significantly more abundant in one habitat type (Supplementary Tables S5).

De plus, pour mieux comprendre les OTU pertinentes pour un habitat donné, nous avons répertorié celles qui ont été trouvées exclusivement dans nos analyses (Tableaux supplémentaires S2, S3 et S4) ou qui étaient significativement plus abondantes dans un type d'habitat (Tableaux supplémentaires S5). ).

tu.prop: Furthermore, to gain a better understanding of the OTUs that are of relevance for a given habitat we listed those that were found in our analyses exclusively (Supplementary Tables S2, S3 and S4) or were significantly more abundant in one habitat type (Supplementary Tables S5).
Nevertheless, the absence in a habitat of an OTU detected in another habitat, is an indication of its rareness.

tuv.seg: As we have shown in a previous study33 apparent habitat endemicity in 16S analyses of the microbial communities for marine environments is in many cases likely to be an artifact of an insufficient coverage so that individuals of very rare OTUs are not detected.

Comme nous l'avons montré dans une étude précédente, l'endémicité apparente de l'habitat dans les analyses 16S des communautés microbiennes des environnements marins est, dans de nombreux cas, susceptible d'être le résultat d'une couverture insuffisante, de sorte que les individus d'OTU très rares ne soient pas détectés.

tu.prop: As we have shown in a previous study33 apparent habitat endemicity in 16S analyses of the microbial communities for marine environments is in many cases likely to be an artifact of an insufficient coverage so that individuals of very rare OTUs are not detected.

Hence, we present the results of both analyses together and refer in the following text to apparently “exclusive” OTUs, as well as OTUs significantly more abundant in a habitat type, as habitat specific OTUs.

tuv.seg: Nevertheless, the absence in a habitat of an OTU detected in another habitat, is an indication of its rareness.
Néanmoins, l'absence, dans un habitat, d'une OTU détectée dans un autre habitat, est une indication de sa rareté.

tu.prop: Nevertheless, the absence in a habitat of an OTU detected in another habitat, is an indication of its rareness.

We present, in the following sections, three comparisons of habitat types: (i) active versus inactive chimneys, (ii) inactive chimneys versus all other samples and (iii) water samples versus all chimneys samples.

tuv.seg: Hence, we present the results of both analyses together and refer in the following text to apparently “exclusive” OTUs, as well as OTUs significantly more abundant in a habitat type, as habitat specific OTUs.

Par conséquent, nous présentons ensemble les résultats des deux analyses et faisons référence dans le texte suivant aux OTU apparemment « exclusives », ainsi qu’aux OTU significativement plus abondantes dans un type d’habitat, en tant qu’OTU spécifiques à un habitat.

tu.prop: Hence, we present the results of both analyses together and refer in the following text to apparently “exclusive” OTUs, as well as OTUs significantly more abundant in a habitat type, as habitat specific OTUs.

tuv.seg: We present, in the following sections, three comparisons of habitat types: (i) active versus inactive chimneys, (ii) inactive chimneys versus all other samples and (iii) water samples versus all chimneys samples.

Nous présentons, dans les sections suivantes, trois comparaisons de types d'habitats : (i) cheminées actives versus inactives, (ii) cheminées inactives versus tous les autres échantillons et (iii) échantillons d'eau versus tous les échantillons de cheminées.

tu.prop: We present, in the following sections, three comparisons of habitat types: (i) active versus inactive chimneys, (ii) inactive chimneys versus all other samples and (iii) water samples versus all chimneys samples.

Our analyses showed a total of 29142 OTUs (13163 and 15979, respectively, in bacterial and archaeal analyses) detected in at least one of the active chimneys but not in the inactive chimneys (Supplementary Table S2).

tuv.seg: Active chimneys versus inactive chimneys
Cheminées actives versus cheminées inactives

tu.prop: Among these active chimney specific OTUs the most abundant ones were classified Epsilonproteobacteria in particular with mesophilic Campylobacterales and thermophilic Nautiliales as well as thermophilic Aquificae.

tuv.seg: Our analyses showed a total of 29142 OTUs (13163 and 15979, respectively, in bacterial and archaeal analyses) detected in at least one of the active chimneys but not in the inactive chimneys (Supplementary Table S2).

Nos analyses ont montré un total de 29 142 OTU (13 163 dans les analyses bactériennes et 15 979 dans celles sur les archées) détectées dans au moins l'une des cheminées actives mais pas dans les cheminées inactives (Tableau S2 supplémentaire).

tu.prop: Our analyses showed a total of 29142 OTUs (13163 and 15979, respectively, in bacterial and archaeal analyses) detected in at least one of the active chimneys but not in the inactive chimneys (Supplementary Table S2).

OTUs significantly more abundant in active chimneys than in inactive chimneys were classified as thermophilic Archaea: Archaeoglobales (sulfate-reducing Archaea)34, Thermococcales (reducing sulfur to H2S)35, Thermoprotei (obtaining energy by oxidation of H2, H2S, and elemental sulfur)36 and Methanococcales (forming methane by CO2 reduction with H2)37 (Supplementary Tables S2,S5).

tuv.seg: Among these active chimney specific OTUs the most abundant ones were classified Epsilonproteobacteria in particular with mesophilic Campylobacterales and thermophilic Nautiliales as well as thermophilic Aquificae.

Parmi ces OTU spécifiques aux cheminées actives, les plus abondantes ont été classées en Epsilonprotéobactéries, en particulier avec des Campylobactérales mésophiles, des Nautiliales thermophiles mais aussi des Aquificae thermophiles.

tu.prop: Among these active chimney specific OTUs the most abundant ones were classified Epsilonproteobacteria in particular with mesophilic Campylobacterales and thermophilic Nautiliales as well as thermophilic Aquificae.
The Bacteria and Archaea found in the Kairei and Pelagia active chimneys are common for this type of habitat1,38,39,40,41,42,43.

tuv.seg: OTUs significantly more abundant in active chimneys than in inactive chimneys were classified as thermophilic Archaea: Archaeoglobales (sulfate-reducing Archaea)34, Thermococcales (reducing sulfur to H2S)35, Thermoprotei (obtaining energy by oxidation of H2, H2S, and elemental sulfur)36 and Methanococcales (forming methane by CO2 reduction with H2)37 (Supplementary Tables S2,S5).

Les OTU significativement plus abondantes dans les cheminées actives que dans les cheminées inactives ont été classées en tant qu'archées thermophiles : Archaeoglobales (archées sulfato-réductrices)[34], Thermococcales (réduisant le soufre en H2S)[35], Thermoprotei (obtenant de l'énergie par oxydation de H2, H2S et soufre élémentaire)37][ et Methanococcales (formant du méthane par réduction du CO2 avec H2)[37] (Tableaux supplémentaires S2, S5).

tu.prop: OTUs significantly more abundant in active chimneys than in inactive chimneys were classified as thermophilic Archaea: Archaeoglobales (sulfate-reducing Archaea)34, Thermococcales (reducing sulfur to H2S)35, Thermoprotei (obtaining energy by oxidation of H2, H2S, and elemental sulfur)36 and Methanococcales (forming methane by CO2 reduction with H2)37 (Supplementary Tables S2,S5).
They are ideally adapted to the thermal and chemical gradients hallmarking the chimney conduit.

tuv.seg: The Bacteria and Archaea found in the Kairei and Pelagia active chimneys are common for this type of habitat1,38,39,40,41,42,43.
Les bactéries et archées trouvées dans les cheminées actives de Kairei et Pelagia sont courantes dans ce type d'habitat[1],[38],[39],[40],[41],[42],[43].

tu.prop: The Bacteria and Archaea found in the Kairei and Pelagia active chimneys are common for this type of habitat1,38,39,40,41,42,43.
The primary producers but also heterotrophs can make use of the readily available reduced compounds provided by the hot, reduced hydrothermal fluids4,44.

tuv.seg: They are ideally adapted to the thermal and chemical gradients hallmarking the chimney conduit.
Elles sont idéalement adaptées aux gradients thermiques et chimiques caractéristiques du conduit de la cheminée.

tu.prop: They are ideally adapted to the thermal and chemical gradients hallmarking the chimney conduit.

Sulfurimonas species, one of the abundant epsilonproteobacterial species in active chimneys, can strongly influence their habitats through autotrophic denitrification coupled with oxidation of sulfides or metal sulfide to soluble metal sulfate45, e.g. remediation of metal sulfide from contaminated sediments46 and remediation of nitrate contamination from groundwater47.

tuv.seg: The primary producers but also heterotrophs can make use of the readily available reduced compounds provided by the hot, reduced hydrothermal fluids4,44.
Les producteurs primaires mais aussi les hétérotrophes peuvent utiliser les composés réduits facilement disponibles fournis par les fluides hydrothermaux chauds et réduits[4],[44].

tu.prop: The primary producers but also heterotrophs can make use of the readily available reduced compounds provided by the hot, reduced hydrothermal fluids4,44.
Many species in the order Nautiliales and Aquificae can reduce element sulfur to produce sulfide4,44,48, resulting in the formation of metal sulfides in their habitats.

tuv.seg: Sulfurimonas species, one of the abundant epsilonproteobacterial species in active chimneys, can strongly influence their habitats through autotrophic denitrification coupled with oxidation of sulfides or metal sulfide to soluble metal sulfate45, e.g. remediation of metal sulfide from contaminated sediments46 and remediation of nitrate contamination from groundwater47.

Les Sulfurimonas, une des principales espèces d'epsilonprotéobactéries dans les cheminées actives, à travers la dénitrification autotrophe associée à l'oxydation des sulfures ou des sulfures métalliques en sulfate métallique soluble[45], peut avoir une forte influence sur leurs habitats, à savoir la décontamination en sulfure métallique des sédiments contaminés[46] et la décontamination en nitrate des eaux souterraines[47].

tu.prop: Sulfurimonas species, one of the abundant epsilonproteobacterial species in active chimneys, can strongly influence their habitats through autotrophic denitrification coupled with oxidation of sulfides or metal sulfide to soluble metal sulfate45, e.g. remediation of metal sulfide from contaminated sediments46 and remediation of nitrate contamination from groundwater47.

Remarkably, most OTUs among active chimney specific Archaea could not be taxonomically classified (besides kingdom level), due to lacking similarity to the sequences in the reference databases (Supplementary Table S2).

tuv.seg: Many species in the order Nautiliales and Aquificae can reduce element sulfur to produce sulfide4,44,48, resulting in the formation of metal sulfides in their habitats.

De nombreuses espèces de l'ordre des Nautiliales et des Aquificae peuvent réduire le soufre élémentaire pour produire du sulfure[4],[44],[48], entraînant ainsi la formation de sulfures métalliques dans leurs habitats.

tu.prop: Many species in the order Nautiliales and Aquificae can reduce element sulfur to produce sulfide4,44,48, resulting in the formation of metal sulfides in their habitats.

Detecting high proportions of previously unknown sequences is not uncommon in such environments38,43: we remain unaware of the role these microbes have for biogeochemical cycling, demonstrating our still quite limited understanding of these settings.

tuv.seg: Remarkably, most OTUs among active chimney specific Archaea could not be taxonomically classified (besides kingdom level), due to lacking similarity to the sequences in the reference databases (Supplementary Table S2).

Il est intéressant de noter que la plupart des OTU (Unités Taxonomiques Opérationnelles) des archées spécifiques aux cheminées actives n'ont pas pu être classées sur le plan taxonomique (en dehors du niveau du règne), faute de similitude avec les séquences répertoriées dans les bases de données de référence (tableau supplémentaire S2).

tu.prop: Remarkably, most OTUs among active chimney specific Archaea could not be taxonomically classified (besides kingdom level), due to lacking similarity to the sequences in the reference databases (Supplementary Table S2).
81002 OTUs (45639 and 35363, respectively, from bacterial and archaeal analyses) were present in at least one of the inactive chimney samples, but not found in active chimneys.

tuv.seg: Detecting high proportions of previously unknown sequences is not uncommon in such environments38,43: we remain unaware of the role these microbes have for biogeochemical cycling, demonstrating our still quite limited understanding of these settings.

Détecter des proportions importantes de séquences inconnues auparavant n'est pas rare dans de tels environnements : le rôle de ces microbes dans les cycles biogéochimiques nous échappe encore, ce qui témoigne de notre compréhension encore très limitée de ces milieux.

tu.prop: Detecting high proportions of previously unknown sequences is not uncommon in such environments38,43: we remain unaware of the role these microbes have for biogeochemical cycling, demonstrating our still quite limited understanding of these settings.

Among the most abundant bacterial OTUs we found phylogenetically distinct groups (Supplementary Table S2): Desulfobulbus, and SAR324 (Deltaproteobacteria), Oceanospirillales, Alteromonadales, Chromatiales (Nitrosoccus and Acidiferrobacter), and Thiotrichales.

tuv.seg: 81002 OTUs (45639 and 35363, respectively, from bacterial and archaeal analyses) were present in at least one of the inactive chimney samples, but not found in active chimneys.

Un total de 81002 OTU ( soit 45639 chez les bactéries et 35363 ches les archées, d'après les analyses) étaient présentes au moins dans un des échantillons provenant de cheminées inactives, mais absentes dans les cheminées actives.

tu.prop: 81002 OTUs (45639 and 35363, respectively, from bacterial and archaeal analyses) were present in at least one of the inactive chimney samples, but not found in active chimneys.
The archaeal OTUs were classified as Woesearchaeota (DHVEG-6, deep-sea hydrothermal vent euryarchaeota group 6), Thaumarchaeota, in particular Nitrosopumilus, and Thermoplasmata (Marine Group III).

tuv.seg: Among the most abundant bacterial OTUs we found phylogenetically distinct groups (Supplementary Table S2): Desulfobulbus, and SAR324 (Deltaproteobacteria), Oceanospirillales, Alteromonadales, Chromatiales (Nitrosoccus and Acidiferrobacter), and Thiotrichales.

Parmi les OTU bactériennes les plus abondantes, nous avons trouvé des groupes phylogénétiquement distincts (tableau supplémentaire S2) : les Desulfobulbus et SAR324 (Deltaproteobacteria), les Oceanospirillales, les Alteromonadales, les Chromatiales (Nitrosoccus et Acidiferrobacter), et les Thiotrichales.

tu.prop: Among the most abundant bacterial OTUs we found phylogenetically distinct groups (Supplementary Table S2): Desulfobulbus, and SAR324 (Deltaproteobacteria), Oceanospirillales, Alteromonadales, Chromatiales (Nitrosoccus and Acidiferrobacter), and Thiotrichales.

Cultured representatives of these microorganisms catalyze ammonia-oxidation49,50,51, sulfide- and/or hydrogen-oxidation52,53,54, can grow autotrophically with iron-sulfide minerals55 and are involved in iron- and sulfur-cycles56,57,58.

tuv.seg: The archaeal OTUs were classified as Woesearchaeota (DHVEG-6, deep-sea hydrothermal vent euryarchaeota group 6), Thaumarchaeota, in particular Nitrosopumilus, and Thermoplasmata (Marine Group III).

Les OTU d'archées ont été classées comme Woesearchaeota (DHVEG-6, groupe 6 des Woesearchaeota des cheminées hydrothermales des fonds marins), Thaumarchaeota, en particulier Nitrosopumilus, et Thermoplasmata (groupe marin III).

tu.prop: The archaeal OTUs were classified as Woesearchaeota (DHVEG-6, deep-sea hydrothermal vent euryarchaeota group 6), Thaumarchaeota, in particular Nitrosopumilus, and Thermoplasmata (Marine Group III).

Affiliates of the Chromatiales can use sulfide minerals such as pyrite as energy source59, while the members of Desulfobacterales can produce extracellular pyrite via reduction of sulfate60,61, indicating that these species can also influence the mineral composition in their habitats.

tuv.seg: Cultured representatives of these microorganisms catalyze ammonia-oxidation49,50,51, sulfide- and/or hydrogen-oxidation52,53,54, can grow autotrophically with iron-sulfide minerals55 and are involved in iron- and sulfur-cycles56,57,58.

En culture, les spécimens de ces microorganismes catalysent l'oxydation de l'ammoniac[49],[50],[51], l'oxydation du sulfure et/ou de l'hydrogène[52],[53],[54] peuvent se développer de façon autotrophe avec des minéraux de sulfure de fer[55] et sont impliqués dans les cycles du fer et du soufre[56],[57],[58].

tu.prop: Cultured representatives of these microorganisms catalyze ammonia-oxidation49,50,51, sulfide- and/or hydrogen-oxidation52,53,54, can grow autotrophically with iron-sulfide minerals55 and are involved in iron- and sulfur-cycles56,57,58.
Nitrosopumilius is an autotrophic ammonia-oxidizing marine archaeon51, which can use very limited amounts of nitrogen and carbon to maintain life62.

tuv.seg: Affiliates of the Chromatiales can use sulfide minerals such as pyrite as energy source59, while the members of Desulfobacterales can produce extracellular pyrite via reduction of sulfate60,61, indicating that these species can also influence the mineral composition in their habitats.

Les espèces du groupe des Chromatiales sont capables d'utiliser des minéraux sulfurés tels que la pyrite comme source d'énergie[59], tandis que les membres du groupe des Desulfobacterales peuvent créer de la pyrite extracellulaire par réduction du sulfate[60],[61], indiquant que ces espèces peuvent également influencer la composition minérale de leurs habitats.

tu.prop: Affiliates of the Chromatiales can use sulfide minerals such as pyrite as energy source59, while the members of Desulfobacterales can produce extracellular pyrite via reduction of sulfate60,61, indicating that these species can also influence the mineral composition in their habitats.
Affiliates of this group have been found in other inactive vents38,63, too, suggesting that its metabolism may play a significant role for nitrogen and carbon cycling in such chimneys62.

tuv.seg: Nitrosopumilius is an autotrophic ammonia-oxidizing marine archaeon51, which can use very limited amounts of nitrogen and carbon to maintain life62.
Le Nitrosopumilius est un archée marin autotrophe oxydant l'ammoniac[51], qui utilise des quantités très limitées d'azote et de carbone pour se maintenir en vie[62].

tu.prop: Nitrosopumilius is an autotrophic ammonia-oxidizing marine archaeon51, which can use very limited amounts of nitrogen and carbon to maintain life62.
Even though some Nitrosopumilius related sequences were also found in active chimneys, the abundance was much lower than in the inactive chimneys in the same field38.

tuv.seg: Affiliates of this group have been found in other inactive vents38,63, too, suggesting that its metabolism may play a significant role for nitrogen and carbon cycling in such chimneys62.

Des espèces de ce groupe ont également été recensées dans d'autres cheminées inactives [38],[63], ce qui suggère que leurs métabolismes pourrait avoir un rôle important dans le cycle de l'azote et du carbone dans ces cheminées [62].

tu.prop: Affiliates of this group have been found in other inactive vents38,63, too, suggesting that its metabolism may play a significant role for nitrogen and carbon cycling in such chimneys62.
The trend of high abundance of Nitrosopumilius related sequences in inactive chimneys coincides with our findings.

tuv.seg: Even though some Nitrosopumilius related sequences were also found in active chimneys, the abundance was much lower than in the inactive chimneys in the same field38.

Bien que certaines séquences apparentées à Nitrosopumilius aient également été découvertes dans des cheminées actives, leur abondance était beaucoup plus faible que dans les cheminées inactives du même site[38].

tu.prop: Even though some Nitrosopumilius related sequences were also found in active chimneys, the abundance was much lower than in the inactive chimneys in the same field38.
In summary, the distinct microbial patterns of the active versus the inactive chimneys confirm that the different chimney environments select for the specific microbial compositions.

tuv.seg: The trend of high abundance of Nitrosopumilius related sequences in inactive chimneys coincides with our findings.
La tendance à forte abondance de séquences apparentées à Nitrosopumilius dans les cheminées inactives concorde avec nos résultats.

tu.prop: The trend of high abundance of Nitrosopumilius related sequences in inactive chimneys coincides with our findings.
Inactive chimneys versus all other samples

tuv.seg: In summary, the distinct microbial patterns of the active versus the inactive chimneys confirm that the different chimney environments select for the specific microbial compositions.

En résumé, les modèles microbiens des cheminées actives distincts de ceux des inactives confirment que les différents habitats des cheminées entraînent une sélection des compositions microbiennes spécifiques.

tu.prop: In summary, the distinct microbial patterns of the active versus the inactive chimneys confirm that the different chimney environments select for the specific microbial compositions.
Some of the inactive chimney OTUs discussed in the previous section, could be absent in active chimneys, but still be present in other kind of habitats.

tuv.seg: Inactive chimneys versus all other samples
Les cheminées inactives comparées à tous les autres échantillons

tu.prop: Inactive chimneys versus all other samples

Our results show that among the 81002 OTUs present in inactive chimneys but not in active chimneys (see previous section), 70625 OTUs were also absent from water, plume and hydrothermal fluid samples (39467 from the bacterial, 31158 from archaeal analysis).

tuv.seg: Some of the inactive chimney OTUs discussed in the previous section, could be absent in active chimneys, but still be present in other kind of habitats.
Certaines des OTU de cheminée inactive évoquées dans la section précédente pourraient être absentes dans les cheminées actives, mais toujours présentes dans d'autres types d'habitats.

tu.prop: Some of the inactive chimney OTUs discussed in the previous section, could be absent in active chimneys, but still be present in other kind of habitats.
The most abundant of these OTUs were classified as Desulfobulbus and members of the Thiotrichales (Supplementary Table S3).

tuv.seg: Our results show that among the 81002 OTUs present in inactive chimneys but not in active chimneys (see previous section), 70625 OTUs were also absent from water, plume and hydrothermal fluid samples (39467 from the bacterial, 31158 from archaeal analysis).

Nos résultats montrent que parmi les 81 002 OTU présentes dans les cheminées inactives mais pas dans les cheminées actives (voir section précédente), 70 625 OTU étaient également absentes des échantillons d'eau, de panache et de fluide hydrothermal (39 467 d'origine bactérienne, 31 158 d'analyse archéenne).

tu.prop: Our results show that among the 81002 OTUs present in inactive chimneys but not in active chimneys (see previous section), 70625 OTUs were also absent from water, plume and hydrothermal fluid samples (39467 from the bacterial, 31158 from archaeal analysis).

Other inactive chimney specific OTUs were scattered across a phylogenetically broad group of taxa including Alpha-, Gamma-, Deltaproteobacteria, Bacteroidetes, Planctomycetes, Actinobacteria, Acidobacteria, Chloroflexi and DHVEG-6, Marine Benthic group E and unassigned Archaea (Supplementary Table S3).

tuv.seg: The most abundant of these OTUs were classified as Desulfobulbus and members of the Thiotrichales (Supplementary Table S3).
Les plus abondantes de ces OTU ont été classées comme Desulfobulbus et membres des Thiotrichales (tableau supplémentaire S3).

tu.prop: The most abundant of these OTUs were classified as Desulfobulbus and members of the Thiotrichales (Supplementary Table S3).
The Marine Benthic group E is frequently detected in deep-sea sediments64 and inactive chimneys9 and placed within the Euryarchaeota.

tuv.seg: Other inactive chimney specific OTUs were scattered across a phylogenetically broad group of taxa including Alpha-, Gamma-, Deltaproteobacteria, Bacteroidetes, Planctomycetes, Actinobacteria, Acidobacteria, Chloroflexi and DHVEG-6, Marine Benthic group E and unassigned Archaea (Supplementary Table S3).

D'autres OTU inactives spécifiques à la cheminée étaient dispersées dans un groupe phylogénétiquement large de taxons comprenant les alpha-, gamma-, deltaprotéobactéries, les bactéroïdes, les planctomycètes, les actinobactéries, les acidobactéries, les chloroflexi et le DHVEG-6, le groupe benthique marin E et les archées non attribuées (tableau supplémentaire S3).

tu.prop: Other inactive chimney specific OTUs were scattered across a phylogenetically broad group of taxa including Alpha-, Gamma-, Deltaproteobacteria, Bacteroidetes, Planctomycetes, Actinobacteria, Acidobacteria, Chloroflexi and DHVEG-6, Marine Benthic group E and unassigned Archaea (Supplementary Table S3).
Their phylogenetic position between members of the Methanobacteriales and the Methanosarcinales may be indicative of a possible methanogenic phenotype65.

tuv.seg: The Marine Benthic group E is frequently detected in deep-sea sediments64 and inactive chimneys9 and placed within the Euryarchaeota.
Le groupe benthique marin E est fréquemment détecté dans les sédiments des grands fonds [64] et les cheminées inactives [9] et placé au sein de l'Euryarchaeota.

tu.prop: The Marine Benthic group E is frequently detected in deep-sea sediments64 and inactive chimneys9 and placed within the Euryarchaeota.
Similar taxonomic patterns have also been found in inactive chimneys before11,38,43.

tuv.seg: Their phylogenetic position between members of the Methanobacteriales and the Methanosarcinales may be indicative of a possible methanogenic phenotype65.
Leur position phylogénétique entre les membres des Methanobacteriales et des Methanosarcinales peut être révélatrice d'un éventuel phénotype méthanogène [65].

tu.prop: Their phylogenetic position between members of the Methanobacteriales and the Methanosarcinales may be indicative of a possible methanogenic phenotype65.
Thus, inactive chimneys support a high microbial diversity and the habitat type may even function as one of the homes to the oceanic seed library.

tuv.seg: Similar taxonomic patterns have also been found in inactive chimneys before11,38,43.
Des modèles taxonomiques similaires ont également été trouvés dans des cheminées inactives auparavant [11],[38],[43].

tu.prop: Similar taxonomic patterns have also been found in inactive chimneys before11,38,43.

Likely, the stability of the inactive chimney environment, i.e. ambient temperatures and slow chemical reaction processes, both weathering but also microbially catalyzed redox reactions, as well as the food sources provided by the chimney mineralogical composition provide an attractive habitat for a broad range of microorganisms.

tuv.seg: Thus, inactive chimneys support a high microbial diversity and the habitat type may even function as one of the homes to the oceanic seed library.
Ainsi, les cheminées inactives abritent une grande diversité microbienne et le type d’habitat peut même servir de foyer à la bibliothèque de graines océaniques.

tu.prop: Thus, inactive chimneys support a high microbial diversity and the habitat type may even function as one of the homes to the oceanic seed library.

tuv.seg: Likely, the stability of the inactive chimney environment, i.e. ambient temperatures and slow chemical reaction processes, both weathering but also microbially catalyzed redox reactions, as well as the food sources provided by the chimney mineralogical composition provide an attractive habitat for a broad range of microorganisms.

Il est probable que la stabilité de l'environnement inactif de la cheminée, c'est-à-dire les températures ambiantes et les processus de réaction chimique lents, à la fois l'altération climatique mais également les réactions redox catalysées par des microbes, ainsi que les sources de nourriture fournies par la composition minéralogique de la cheminée fournissent un habitat attrayant pour un large éventail de micro-organismes.
