tu.prop: Likely, the stability of the inactive chimney environment, i.e. ambient temperatures and slow chemical reaction processes, both weathering but also microbially catalyzed redox reactions, as well as the food sources provided by the chimney mineralogical composition provide an attractive habitat for a broad range of microorganisms.
16153 bacterial and 22094 archaeal OTUs were found in at least one of the open ocean samples (RF1 and RF4) but not in any of the chimney samples (Supplementary Table S4).

tuv.seg: Open ocean versus hydrothermal samples
Échantillons en zone pélagique et échantillons hydrothermaux

tu.prop: Among OTUs detected in both habitats, several significant differences were also noted (Supplementary Tables S4 and S5): some of the major differences were related to water samples containing larger proportions of heterotrophic Oceanospirillales and Alteromonadales (Gammaproteobacteria) and SAR324 (Deltaproteobacteria).

tuv.seg: 16153 bacterial and 22094 archaeal OTUs were found in at least one of the open ocean samples (RF1 and RF4) but not in any of the chimney samples (Supplementary Table S4).

16 153 Des OTU bactériennes et 22 094 archéennes ont été trouvées dans au moins un des échantillons en zone pélagique (RF1 et RF4), mais dans aucun des échantillons de cheminée (tableau supplémentaire S4).

tu.prop: 16153 bacterial and 22094 archaeal OTUs were found in at least one of the open ocean samples (RF1 and RF4) but not in any of the chimney samples (Supplementary Table S4).
Oceanospirillales and Alteromonadales have been repetitively found in ocean water samples58,66,67,68,69.

tuv.seg: Among OTUs detected in both habitats, several significant differences were also noted (Supplementary Tables S4 and S5): some of the major differences were related to water samples containing larger proportions of heterotrophic Oceanospirillales and Alteromonadales (Gammaproteobacteria) and SAR324 (Deltaproteobacteria).

Parmi les OTU détectées dans les deux habitats, plusieurs différences significatives ont également été notées (Tableaux supplémentaires S4 et S5) : certaines des différences majeures étaient liées aux échantillons d'eau contenant de plus grandes proportions d'Océanospirillales et d'Alteromonadales hétérotrophes (Gammaprotéobactéries) et de SAR324 (Deltaprotéobactéries).

tu.prop: Among OTUs detected in both habitats, several significant differences were also noted (Supplementary Tables S4 and S5): some of the major differences were related to water samples containing larger proportions of heterotrophic Oceanospirillales and Alteromonadales (Gammaproteobacteria) and SAR324 (Deltaproteobacteria).

Versatile metabolisms (lithotrophy and heterotrophy) for SAR324 make these organisms ubiquitous in the global marine biosphere58, as in water samples58 (and this study), inactive chimneys (this study), hydrothermal plume58,70 and sediments71.

tuv.seg: Oceanospirillales and Alteromonadales have been repetitively found in ocean water samples58,66,67,68,69.
Des océanospirillales et des alteromonadales ont été trouvées à plusieurs reprises dans des échantillons d'eau océanique [58],[66],[67],[68],[69].

tu.prop: Oceanospirillales and Alteromonadales have been repetitively found in ocean water samples58,66,67,68,69.
Members of the Thaumarchaeota Marine group I, which are typically associated with oxic marine coastal waters72,73, dominated much of the archaeal communities in water samples.

tuv.seg: Versatile metabolisms (lithotrophy and heterotrophy) for SAR324 make these organisms ubiquitous in the global marine biosphere58, as in water samples58 (and this study), inactive chimneys (this study), hydrothermal plume58,70 and sediments71.

Les métabolismes polyvalents (lithotrophie et hétérotrophie) du SAR324 rendent ces organismes omniprésents dans la biosphère marine mondiale [58], comme dans les échantillons d'eau [58] (et cette étude), les cheminées inactives (cette étude), le panache hydrothermal [58],[70] et les sédiments [71].

tu.prop: Versatile metabolisms (lithotrophy and heterotrophy) for SAR324 make these organisms ubiquitous in the global marine biosphere58, as in water samples58 (and this study), inactive chimneys (this study), hydrothermal plume58,70 and sediments71.
Phototrophic Cyanobacteria and affiliates of the SAR11 clade and SAR116 clade (Alphaproteobacteria) have been found in water samples as well74,75 and were also detected in our water samples.

tuv.seg: Members of the Thaumarchaeota Marine group I, which are typically associated with oxic marine coastal waters72,73, dominated much of the archaeal communities in water samples.

Les membres du groupe Thaumarchaeota Marine I, qui sont généralement associés aux eaux côtières marines oxiques [72],[73], dominaient une grande partie des communautés archéennes dans les échantillons d'eau.

tu.prop: Members of the Thaumarchaeota Marine group I, which are typically associated with oxic marine coastal waters72,73, dominated much of the archaeal communities in water samples.

These species can perform photosynthesis and consume organic carbons, while most dominant species in hydrothermal fields are chemolithotrophs, indicating that the different environmental conditions between open oceans and hydrothermal vents select the species according to their physiological characteristics and result in different diversities among these habitats.

tuv.seg: Phototrophic Cyanobacteria and affiliates of the SAR11 clade and SAR116 clade (Alphaproteobacteria) have been found in water samples as well74,75 and were also detected in our water samples.

Des cyanobactéries phototrophes et des affiliés des clades SAR11 et SAR116 (Alphaprotéobactéries) ont également été trouvés dans des échantillons d'eau [74],[75] et ont également été détectés dans nos échantillons d'eau.

tu.prop: Phototrophic Cyanobacteria and affiliates of the SAR11 clade and SAR116 clade (Alphaproteobacteria) have been found in water samples as well74,75 and were also detected in our water samples.
Linking mineralogy with the abundance of Bacteria and Archaea

tuv.seg: These species can perform photosynthesis and consume organic carbons, while most dominant species in hydrothermal fields are chemolithotrophs, indicating that the different environmental conditions between open oceans and hydrothermal vents select the species according to their physiological characteristics and result in different diversities among these habitats.

Ces espèces peuvent effectuer la photosynthèse et consommer du carbone organique, tandis que la plupart des espèces dominantes dans les champs hydrothermaux sont des chimiolithotrophes, ce qui indique que les différentes conditions environnementales entre les océans ouverts et les sources hydrothermales sélectionnent les espèces en fonction de leurs caractéristiques physiologiques et entraînent différentes diversités entre ces habitats.

tu.prop: These species can perform photosynthesis and consume organic carbons, while most dominant species in hydrothermal fields are chemolithotrophs, indicating that the different environmental conditions between open oceans and hydrothermal vents select the species according to their physiological characteristics and result in different diversities among these habitats.
Our tested active and inactive chimneys in both fields are rich in two or more metal sulfides (Table 1).

tuv.seg: Linking mineralogy with the abundance of Bacteria and Archaea
Relier la minéralogie à l’abondance de bactéries et d’archées

tu.prop: Linking mineralogy with the abundance of Bacteria and Archaea

The formation of these minerals and degree of rock alteration depend on the temperature and the chemical composition of hydrothermal fluids as well as the age of chimneys, respectively76, explaining the distinct mineralogical composition in the tested chimneys (Table 1).

tuv.seg: Our tested active and inactive chimneys in both fields are rich in two or more metal sulfides (Table 1).
Nos cheminées actives et inactives testées dans les deux domaines sont riches en deux ou plusieurs sulfures métalliques (Tableau 1).

tu.prop: Our tested active and inactive chimneys in both fields are rich in two or more metal sulfides (Table 1).

To examine whether the mineral compositions influence the bacterial and archaeal diversities, the proportions of chalcopyrite, pyrite and sphalerite relative to the microbial community compositions were analyzed (Supplementary Table S6).

tuv.seg: The formation of these minerals and degree of rock alteration depend on the temperature and the chemical composition of hydrothermal fluids as well as the age of chimneys, respectively76, explaining the distinct mineralogical composition in the tested chimneys (Table 1).

La formation de ces minéraux et le degré d'altération des roches dépendent respectivement de la température et de la composition chimique des fluides hydrothermaux ainsi que de l'âge des cheminées76, expliquant la composition minéralogique distincte des cheminées testées (tableau 1).

tu.prop: The formation of these minerals and degree of rock alteration depend on the temperature and the chemical composition of hydrothermal fluids as well as the age of chimneys, respectively76, explaining the distinct mineralogical composition in the tested chimneys (Table 1).
Generally, no significant difference between the appearance of some of the dominant microbes and mineral compositions could be established (Supplementary Table S6).

tuv.seg: To examine whether the mineral compositions influence the bacterial and archaeal diversities, the proportions of chalcopyrite, pyrite and sphalerite relative to the microbial community compositions were analyzed (Supplementary Table S6).

Pour examiner si les compositions minérales influencent les diversités bactériennes et archéennes, les proportions de chalcopyrite, de pyrite et de sphalérite par rapport aux compositions de la communauté microbienne ont été analysées (tableau supplémentaire S6).

tu.prop: To examine whether the mineral compositions influence the bacterial and archaeal diversities, the proportions of chalcopyrite, pyrite and sphalerite relative to the microbial community compositions were analyzed (Supplementary Table S6).
Consequently, metal contents (alone) do not appear to play a primary role for microbial community compositions.

tuv.seg: Generally, no significant difference between the appearance of some of the dominant microbes and mineral compositions could be established (Supplementary Table S6).
En règle générale, aucune différence significative entre l'apparence de certains des microbes dominants et la composition minérale n'a pu être établie (tableau supplémentaire S6).

tu.prop: Generally, no significant difference between the appearance of some of the dominant microbes and mineral compositions could be established (Supplementary Table S6).

However, based on the inferred metabolisms from the identified microbes the availability of distinct sulfur species appears to be relevant: Sulfurimonas, Sulfurovum and Nitratifractor use reduced sulfur compounds as electron donors4,45, Desulfobulbus and Archaeoglobi are sulfate-reducers34,57,61, and Thermoplasmata can reduce sulfur to sulfide77.

tuv.seg: Consequently, metal contents (alone) do not appear to play a primary role for microbial community compositions.
Par conséquent, la teneur en métaux (seule) ne semble pas jouer un rôle primordial dans la composition de la communauté microbienne.

tu.prop: Consequently, metal contents (alone) do not appear to play a primary role for microbial community compositions.
Hence, besides temperature, the type of sulfur species appear to be one major environmental selective factor for shaping the microbial chimney communities.

tuv.seg: However, based on the inferred metabolisms from the identified microbes the availability of distinct sulfur species appears to be relevant: Sulfurimonas, Sulfurovum and Nitratifractor use reduced sulfur compounds as electron donors4,45, Desulfobulbus and Archaeoglobi are sulfate-reducers34,57,61, and Thermoplasmata can reduce sulfur to sulfide77.

Cependant, sur la base des métabolismes déduits des microbes identifiés, la disponibilité d'espèces soufrées distinctes semble pertinente : Sulfurimonas, Sulfurovum et Nitratifractor utilisent des composés soufrés réduits comme donneurs d'électrons4,45, Desulfobulbus et Archaeoglobi sont des réducteurs de sulfate34,57,61 et les thermoplasmes peuvent réduire le soufre en sulfure77.

tu.prop: However, based on the inferred metabolisms from the identified microbes the availability of distinct sulfur species appears to be relevant: Sulfurimonas, Sulfurovum and Nitratifractor use reduced sulfur compounds as electron donors4,45, Desulfobulbus and Archaeoglobi are sulfate-reducers34,57,61, and Thermoplasmata can reduce sulfur to sulfide77.
This is also supported by previous findings where different microbial communities and mineral compositions from inactive chimneys in the Iheya North where compared to those from the Kairei fields9.

tuv.seg: Hence, besides temperature, the type of sulfur species appear to be one major environmental selective factor for shaping the microbial chimney communities.
Par conséquent, outre la température, le type d’espèces soufrées semble être un facteur sélectif environnemental majeur pour façonner les communautés microbiennes des cheminées.

tu.prop: Hence, besides temperature, the type of sulfur species appear to be one major environmental selective factor for shaping the microbial chimney communities.
Major minerals in Iheya North are sulfate minerals (barite), while the Kairei field is still nascent and composed of reduced sulfide minerals (chalcopyrite)9.

tuv.seg: This is also supported by previous findings where different microbial communities and mineral compositions from inactive chimneys in the Iheya North where compared to those from the Kairei fields9.

Ceci est également étayé par des découvertes antérieures où les différentes communautés microbiennes et compositions minérales des cheminées inactives du nord d'Iheya étaient comparées à celles des champs de Kairei9.

tu.prop: This is also supported by previous findings where different microbial communities and mineral compositions from inactive chimneys in the Iheya North where compared to those from the Kairei fields9.
The reduced status of sulfur in the Kairei field appears to result in a higher diversity of microbial communities than in the Iheya North samples9.

tuv.seg: Major minerals in Iheya North are sulfate minerals (barite), while the Kairei field is still nascent and composed of reduced sulfide minerals (chalcopyrite)9.
Les principaux minéraux d'Iheya du Nord sont des minéraux sulfatés (barytine), tandis que le champ de Kairei est encore naissant et composé de minéraux sulfurés réduits (chalcopyrite)9.

tu.prop: Major minerals in Iheya North are sulfate minerals (barite), while the Kairei field is still nascent and composed of reduced sulfide minerals (chalcopyrite)9.
Specificity and relatedness of the different Kairei and Pelagia communities

tuv.seg: The reduced status of sulfur in the Kairei field appears to result in a higher diversity of microbial communities than in the Iheya North samples9.
Le statut réduit du soufre dans le champ de Kairei semble entraîner une plus grande diversité de communautés microbiennes que dans les échantillons d'Iheya du Nord9.

tu.prop: The reduced status of sulfur in the Kairei field appears to result in a higher diversity of microbial communities than in the Iheya North samples9.

The UPGMA clustering of the samples, based on beta diversity analyses, yielded quite different results depending on whether de novo and/or reference-based OTUs were considered and whether the Sørensen-Dice or Bray-Curtis index was used for analyses (Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: Specificity and relatedness of the different Kairei and Pelagia communities
Spécificité et parenté des différentes communautés Kairei et Pelagia

tu.prop: Specificity and relatedness of the different Kairei and Pelagia communities
In all analyses water samples always clustered as one group indicative of these samples being clearly different to all the other investigated habitats.

tuv.seg: The UPGMA clustering of the samples, based on beta diversity analyses, yielded quite different results depending on whether de novo and/or reference-based OTUs were considered and whether the Sørensen-Dice or Bray-Curtis index was used for analyses (Fig. 1).

Le regroupement UPGMA des échantillons, basé sur des analyses de diversité bêta, a donné des résultats assez différents selon que les OTU de novo et/ou de référence ont été prises en compte et que l'indice de Sørensen-Dice ou de Bray-Curtis a été utilisé pour les analyses (Fig. 1).

tu.prop: The UPGMA clustering of the samples, based on beta diversity analyses, yielded quite different results depending on whether de novo and/or reference-based OTUs were considered and whether the Sørensen-Dice or Bray-Curtis index was used for analyses (Fig. 1).

Interestingly enough, only analyses of Bacteria (if sequence counts were considered) (Fig. 1a) and of Archaea (regardless of whether sequence counts were considered or not) (Fig. 1g,j) led to clustering of active versus inactive (including sulfide talus) chimney communities if sequences of de novo OTUs were used.

tuv.seg: In all analyses water samples always clustered as one group indicative of these samples being clearly different to all the other investigated habitats.
Dans toutes les analyses, les échantillons d'eau ont toujours été regroupés en un seul groupe, ce qui indique que ces échantillons sont clairement différents de tous les autres habitats étudiés.

tu.prop: In all analyses water samples always clustered as one group indicative of these samples being clearly different to all the other investigated habitats.
It is the same case for Archaea if sequences of all OTUs were used and sequence counts were considered (Fig. 1h), but not if computed using only sequences of reference-based OTUs (Fig. 1i,l).

tuv.seg: Interestingly enough, only analyses of Bacteria (if sequence counts were considered) (Fig. 1a) and of Archaea (regardless of whether sequence counts were considered or not) (Fig. 1g,j) led to clustering of active versus inactive (including sulfide talus) chimney communities if sequences of de novo OTUs were used.

Il est intéressant de noter que seules les analyses des bactéries (si le nombre de séquences étaient pris en compte) (Fig. 1a) et des Archées (que le nombre de séquences ait été pris en compte ou non) (Fig. 1g, j) ont conduit à un regroupement des actifs et des inactifs (y compris les talus de sulfures) communautés de cheminées si des séquences d’OTU de novo étaient utilisées.

tu.prop: Interestingly enough, only analyses of Bacteria (if sequence counts were considered) (Fig. 1a) and of Archaea (regardless of whether sequence counts were considered or not) (Fig. 1g,j) led to clustering of active versus inactive (including sulfide talus) chimney communities if sequences of de novo OTUs were used.

Consequently, the de novo OTUs, for which we lack knowledge of possible metabolisms and bio-geo coupling processes, and which in some cases made up a large proportions of the community (up to 99%), have a profound effect on segregating for a habitat specific community.

tuv.seg: It is the same case for Archaea if sequences of all OTUs were used and sequence counts were considered (Fig. 1h), but not if computed using only sequences of reference-based OTUs (Fig. 1i,l).

Il en va de même pour les archées si les séquences de toutes les OTU étaient utilisées et si le décompte des séquences était pris en compte (Fig. 1h), mais pas s'il était calculé en utilisant uniquement des séquences d'OTU basées sur une référence (Fig. 1i, l).

tu.prop: It is the same case for Archaea if sequences of all OTUs were used and sequence counts were considered (Fig. 1h), but not if computed using only sequences of reference-based OTUs (Fig. 1i,l).
To place our samples into context with communities inhabiting other marine habitats, we assessed a total of 37,038,638 bacterial and 6,564,461 archaeal 16S rRNA amplicons.

tuv.seg: Consequently, the de novo OTUs, for which we lack knowledge of possible metabolisms and bio-geo coupling processes, and which in some cases made up a large proportions of the community (up to 99%), have a profound effect on segregating for a habitat specific community.

Par conséquent, les OTU de novo, pour lesquelles nous manquons de connaissances sur les métabolismes possibles et les processus de bio-géocouplage, et qui dans certains cas représentaient une grande proportion de la communauté (jusqu'à 99 %), ont un effet profond sur la ségrégation d'une communauté spécifique à un habitat.

tu.prop: Consequently, the de novo OTUs, for which we lack knowledge of possible metabolisms and bio-geo coupling processes, and which in some cases made up a large proportions of the community (up to 99%), have a profound effect on segregating for a habitat specific community.

Hereby we considered only reference-based OTUs (in order to allow comparison of studies with results from different regions of the 16S rRNA gene) taxonomically assigned to Bacteria or Archaea, respectively (in order to include results from studies using universal primers).

tuv.seg: To place our samples into context with communities inhabiting other marine habitats, we assessed a total of 37,038,638 bacterial and 6,564,461 archaeal 16S rRNA amplicons.

Pour placer nos échantillons dans le contexte des communautés habitant d’autres habitats marins, nous avons évalué un total de 37 038 638 amplicons bactériens et 6 564 461 amplicons d’ARNr 16S archéens.

tu.prop: To place our samples into context with communities inhabiting other marine habitats, we assessed a total of 37,038,638 bacterial and 6,564,461 archaeal 16S rRNA amplicons.

These sequences were from open ocean samples in the Atlantic33, the Labrador Sea78, hydrothermal fluids and biofilms from chimneys along the Mid-Atlantic Ridge (MAR)33,79, an active venting chimney from the Arctic mid-ocean ridge (MOR)80, mineral deposits from the MAR and the Pacific81, inactive chimneys from the East Pacific Rise (EPR)10, sediments near an active vent on the MAR82, sediments from the bathypelagic near the MAR82, methane seep83 and sediments from an inactive hydrothermal venting site on the southwest Indian ridge (SWIR)84.

tuv.seg: Hereby we considered only reference-based OTUs (in order to allow comparison of studies with results from different regions of the 16S rRNA gene) taxonomically assigned to Bacteria or Archaea, respectively (in order to include results from studies using universal primers).

Par la présente, nous avons considéré uniquement les OTU basées sur des références (afin de permettre la comparaison des études avec les résultats de différentes régions du gène de l'ARNr 16S) attribuées taxonomiquement aux bactéries ou aux archées, respectivement (afin d'inclure les résultats d'études utilisant des amorces universelles).

tu.prop: Hereby we considered only reference-based OTUs (in order to allow comparison of studies with results from different regions of the 16S rRNA gene) taxonomically assigned to Bacteria or Archaea, respectively (in order to include results from studies using universal primers).
PCo analyses showed that bacterial communities from the Kairei and Pelagia chimneys were distinctly different to those from other MOR samples (Fig. 2a).

tuv.seg: These sequences were from open ocean samples in the Atlantic33, the Labrador Sea78, hydrothermal fluids and biofilms from chimneys along the Mid-Atlantic Ridge (MAR)33,79, an active venting chimney from the Arctic mid-ocean ridge (MOR)80, mineral deposits from the MAR and the Pacific81, inactive chimneys from the East Pacific Rise (EPR)10, sediments near an active vent on the MAR82, sediments from the bathypelagic near the MAR82, methane seep83 and sediments from an inactive hydrothermal venting site on the southwest Indian ridge (SWIR)84.

Ces séquences provenaient d'échantillons en zone pélagique dans l'Atlantique33, dans la mer du Labrador78, de fluides hydrothermaux et de biofilms provenant de cheminées le long de la dorsale médio-atlantique (MAR)33,79, d'une cheminée de ventilation active de la dorsale médio-océanique arctique (MOR)80, gisements minéraux du MAR et du Pacifique81, cheminées inactives de l'East Pacific Rise (EPR)10, sédiments à proximité d'un évent actif sur le MAR82, sédiments de la bathypélagique à proximité du MAR82, suintements de méthane83 et sédiments d'un site de ventilation hydrothermale inactif sur le MAR82. crête indienne sud-ouest (SWIR)84.

tu.prop: These sequences were from open ocean samples in the Atlantic33, the Labrador Sea78, hydrothermal fluids and biofilms from chimneys along the Mid-Atlantic Ridge (MAR)33,79, an active venting chimney from the Arctic mid-ocean ridge (MOR)80, mineral deposits from the MAR and the Pacific81, inactive chimneys from the East Pacific Rise (EPR)10, sediments near an active vent on the MAR82, sediments from the bathypelagic near the MAR82, methane seep83 and sediments from an inactive hydrothermal venting site on the southwest Indian ridge (SWIR)84.

When taking sequence abundance into account, there is a general trend of all analyzed bacterial communities from hydrothermally influenced environments to form one distinct cluster while those of the non-hydrothermally influenced samples are scattered outside of this group (Fig. 2b).

tuv.seg: PCo analyses showed that bacterial communities from the Kairei and Pelagia chimneys were distinctly different to those from other MOR samples (Fig. 2a).
Les analyses PCo ont démontré que les communautés bactériennes des cheminées Kairei et Pelagia étaient nettement différentes de celles des autres échantillons MOR (Fig. 2a).

tu.prop: PCo analyses showed that bacterial communities from the Kairei and Pelagia chimneys were distinctly different to those from other MOR samples (Fig. 2a).
However, the inactive chimneys S1 and S7 and to a lesser extent the sulfide talus S3 do not follow this tendency but actually cluster with bacteria from active venting regimes.

tuv.seg: When taking sequence abundance into account, there is a general trend of all analyzed bacterial communities from hydrothermally influenced environments to form one distinct cluster while those of the non-hydrothermally influenced samples are scattered outside of this group (Fig. 2b).

Lorsque l'on prend en compte l'abondance des séquences, il existe une tendance générale de toutes les communautés bactériennes analysées provenant d'environnements influencés par l'hydrothermie à former un groupe distinct, tandis que celles des échantillons non influencés par l'hydrothermie sont dispersées en dehors de ce groupe (Fig. 2b).

tu.prop: When taking sequence abundance into account, there is a general trend of all analyzed bacterial communities from hydrothermally influenced environments to form one distinct cluster while those of the non-hydrothermally influenced samples are scattered outside of this group (Fig. 2b).
Given that inactive chimneys S1 and S7 were sampled from active venting zones and the sulfide talus S3 was collected in a diffuse venting area this is not too surprising.

tuv.seg: However, the inactive chimneys S1 and S7 and to a lesser extent the sulfide talus S3 do not follow this tendency but actually cluster with bacteria from active venting regimes.

Cependant, les cheminées inactives S1 et S7 et, dans une moindre mesure, les talus sulfurés S3 ne suivent pas cette tendance mais se regroupent en fait avec des bactéries provenant de régimes de ventilation actifs.

tu.prop: However, the inactive chimneys S1 and S7 and to a lesser extent the sulfide talus S3 do not follow this tendency but actually cluster with bacteria from active venting regimes.

Bacterial communities of the inactive chimneys S2 and S4 from the inactive zone, cluster together with those from inactive sulfide chimneys from the EPR and a sediment sample from an inactive venting region on the SWIR (Fig. 2b).

tuv.seg: Given that inactive chimneys S1 and S7 were sampled from active venting zones and the sulfide talus S3 was collected in a diffuse venting area this is not too surprising.

Étant donné que les cheminées inactives S1 et S7 ont été échantillonnées dans des zones de ventilation actives et que les talus de sulfures S3 ont été collectés dans une zone de ventilation diffuse, cela n'est pas trop surprenant.

tu.prop: Given that inactive chimneys S1 and S7 were sampled from active venting zones and the sulfide talus S3 was collected in a diffuse venting area this is not too surprising.

According to the Sørensen-Dice index, the archaeal communities of inactive chimneys and the sulfide talus sample appeared scattered but isolated from the other mineral deposits and hydrothermally influenced samples (Fig. 2c).

tuv.seg: Bacterial communities of the inactive chimneys S2 and S4 from the inactive zone, cluster together with those from inactive sulfide chimneys from the EPR and a sediment sample from an inactive venting region on the SWIR (Fig. 2b).

Les communautés bactériennes des cheminées inactives S2 et S4 de la zone inactive se regroupent avec celles des cheminées de sulfures inactives de l'EPR et un échantillon de sédiments provenant d'une région de ventilation inactive du SWIR (Fig. 2b).

tu.prop: Bacterial communities of the inactive chimneys S2 and S4 from the inactive zone, cluster together with those from inactive sulfide chimneys from the EPR and a sediment sample from an inactive venting region on the SWIR (Fig. 2b).
Active chimneys though clustered with other active venting deposits from geographically distinct locations (Fig. 2c).

tuv.seg: According to the Sørensen-Dice index, the archaeal communities of inactive chimneys and the sulfide talus sample appeared scattered but isolated from the other mineral deposits and hydrothermally influenced samples (Fig. 2c).

Selon l'indice de Sørensen-Dice, les communautés archéennes de cheminées inactives et l'échantillon d'éboulis de sulfures semblaient dispersés mais isolés des autres gisements minéraux et des échantillons influencés par l'hydrothermie (Fig. 2c).

tu.prop: According to the Sørensen-Dice index, the archaeal communities of inactive chimneys and the sulfide talus sample appeared scattered but isolated from the other mineral deposits and hydrothermally influenced samples (Fig. 2c).
When quantitative measures were applied only S1 was isolated while S4 grouped with non-hydrothermal samples and S7 clustered with hydrothermally influenced habitats (Fig. 2d).

tuv.seg: Active chimneys though clustered with other active venting deposits from geographically distinct locations (Fig. 2c).
Les cheminées actives, mais regroupées avec d'autres dépôts de ventilation actifs provenant d'emplacements géographiquement distincts (Fig. 2c).

tu.prop: Active chimneys though clustered with other active venting deposits from geographically distinct locations (Fig. 2c).
It appears that different parameters are shaping bacterial and archaeal communities.

tuv.seg: When quantitative measures were applied only S1 was isolated while S4 grouped with non-hydrothermal samples and S7 clustered with hydrothermally influenced habitats (Fig. 2d).

Lorsque des mesures quantitatives ont été appliquées, seul S1 a été isolé, tandis que S4 était regroupé avec des échantillons non hydrothermaux et S7 avec des habitats influencés par l'hydrothermie (Fig. 2d).

tu.prop: When quantitative measures were applied only S1 was isolated while S4 grouped with non-hydrothermal samples and S7 clustered with hydrothermally influenced habitats (Fig. 2d).
Rare microbial taxa and unclassified microorganisms of the Kairei and Pelagia areas

tuv.seg: It appears that different parameters are shaping bacterial and archaeal communities.
Il semble que différents paramètres façonnent les communautés bactériennes et archéennes.

tu.prop: It appears that different parameters are shaping bacterial and archaeal communities.
Due to the depth of sequencing provided by the current high-throughput sequencing technologies85,86, it is now routine to detect rare microbial taxa (relative abundance <0.1% of total community85).

tuv.seg: Rare microbial taxa and unclassified microorganisms of the Kairei and Pelagia areas
Taxons microbiens rares et micro-organismes non classés des zones de Kairei et Pelagia

tu.prop: Rare microbial taxa and unclassified microorganisms of the Kairei and Pelagia areas
Rarity is likely a consequence of multiple environmental factors18.

tuv.seg: Due to the depth of sequencing provided by the current high-throughput sequencing technologies85,86, it is now routine to detect rare microbial taxa (relative abundance <0.1% of total community85).

En raison de la profondeur du séquençage fourni par les technologies actuelles de séquençage à haut débit85,86, il est désormais courant de détecter des taxons microbiens rares (abondance relative <0,1 % de la communauté totale85).

tu.prop: Due to the depth of sequencing provided by the current high-throughput sequencing technologies85,86, it is now routine to detect rare microbial taxa (relative abundance <0.1% of total community85).

Previous studies have demonstrated that rare marine species exhibit different activity over time87: the rare biosphere in old hydrothermal chimneys can become dominant if environmental conditions change88 illustrating that the rare biosphere in a marine ecosystem is dynamic over time89.

tuv.seg: Rarity is likely a consequence of multiple environmental factors18.
La rareté est probablement la conséquence de multiples facteurs environnementaux18.

tu.prop: Rarity is likely a consequence of multiple environmental factors18.

Rare species among Rhodobacterales and Rhizobiales (Alphaproteobacteria) and Desulfobacterales (Deltaproteobacteria) and two rare species associated with the archaeal class Thermoprotei were identified “exclusively” in our chimney samples (Supplementary Table S7).

tuv.seg: Previous studies have demonstrated that rare marine species exhibit different activity over time87: the rare biosphere in old hydrothermal chimneys can become dominant if environmental conditions change88 illustrating that the rare biosphere in a marine ecosystem is dynamic over time89.

Des études antérieures ont démontré que les espèces marines rares présentent une activité différente au fil du temps87 : la biosphère rare dans les anciennes cheminées hydrothermales peut devenir dominante si les conditions environnementales changent88, illustrant que la biosphère rare dans un écosystème marin est dynamique au fil du temps89.

tu.prop: Previous studies have demonstrated that rare marine species exhibit different activity over time87: the rare biosphere in old hydrothermal chimneys can become dominant if environmental conditions change88 illustrating that the rare biosphere in a marine ecosystem is dynamic over time89.
These species are mainly involved in sulfur and metal cycling36,90,91,92.

tuv.seg: Rare species among Rhodobacterales and Rhizobiales (Alphaproteobacteria) and Desulfobacterales (Deltaproteobacteria) and two rare species associated with the archaeal class Thermoprotei were identified “exclusively” in our chimney samples (Supplementary Table S7).

Des espèces rares parmi les Rhodobacterales et Rhizobiales (Alphaproteobacteria) et Desulfobacterales (Deltaproteobacteria) et deux espèces rares associées à la classe des archées Thermoprotei ont été identifiées « exclusivement » dans nos échantillons de cheminée (tableau supplémentaire S7).

tu.prop: Rare species among Rhodobacterales and Rhizobiales (Alphaproteobacteria) and Desulfobacterales (Deltaproteobacteria) and two rare species associated with the archaeal class Thermoprotei were identified “exclusively” in our chimney samples (Supplementary Table S7).
Given the prevalence of massive metal sulfides in the Kairei and Pelagia fields, the metabolisms of these rare species match local environmental features.

tuv.seg: These species are mainly involved in sulfur and metal cycling36,90,91,92.
Ces espèces sont principalement impliquées dans le cycle du soufre et des métaux36,90,91,92.

tu.prop: These species are mainly involved in sulfur and metal cycling36,90,91,92.
Rare species from the bacterial class Nitrospira93 were also detected in our samples (Supplementary Table S7) and in other inactive vents in the southwest India Ridge84.

tuv.seg: Given the prevalence of massive metal sulfides in the Kairei and Pelagia fields, the metabolisms of these rare species match local environmental features.
Compte tenu de la prévalence de sulfures métalliques massifs dans les champs de Kairei et Pelagia, les métabolismes de ces espèces rares correspondent aux caractéristiques environnementales locales.

tu.prop: Given the prevalence of massive metal sulfides in the Kairei and Pelagia fields, the metabolisms of these rare species match local environmental features.
They can perform complete denitrification and play an important role in nitrogen cycling93.

tuv.seg: Rare species from the bacterial class Nitrospira93 were also detected in our samples (Supplementary Table S7) and in other inactive vents in the southwest India Ridge84.
Des espèces rares de la classe bactérienne Nitrospira93 ont également été détectées dans nos échantillons (tableau supplémentaire S7) et dans d'autres évents inactifs du sud-ouest de l'Inde Ridge84.

tu.prop: Rare species from the bacterial class Nitrospira93 were also detected in our samples (Supplementary Table S7) and in other inactive vents in the southwest India Ridge84.
Such rare biosphere is supposed to store ecological potential and facilitate a seed library of metabolisms.

tuv.seg: They can perform complete denitrification and play an important role in nitrogen cycling93.
Ils peuvent effectuer une dénitrification totale et jouer un rôle important dans le cycle de l’azote93.

tu.prop: They can perform complete denitrification and play an important role in nitrogen cycling93.
They may become active and predominant if environmental conditions become favorable with respect to their growth requirements85,86.

tuv.seg: Such rare biosphere is supposed to store ecological potential and facilitate a seed library of metabolisms.
Une biosphère aussi rare est censée stocker un potentiel écologique et faciliter une bibliothèque de semences de métabolismes.

tu.prop: Such rare biosphere is supposed to store ecological potential and facilitate a seed library of metabolisms.
Rare species are expected to be dormant, reducing their own activity/energy requirement, as a survival mechanism, to deal with the current unfavorable growth conditions87.

tuv.seg: They may become active and predominant if environmental conditions become favorable with respect to their growth requirements85,86.
Ils peuvent devenir actifs et prédominants si les conditions environnementales deviennent favorables par rapport à leurs besoins de croissance85,86.

tu.prop: They may become active and predominant if environmental conditions become favorable with respect to their growth requirements85,86.
They can be considered “losers” in the competition for nutrients during one certain time period87 and become “winners” at a later time point.

tuv.seg: Rare species are expected to be dormant, reducing their own activity/energy requirement, as a survival mechanism, to deal with the current unfavorable growth conditions87.

Les espèces rares devraient être en dormance, réduisant ainsi leur propre activité/besoin énergétique, en tant que mécanisme de survie, pour faire face aux conditions de croissance défavorables actuelles87.

tu.prop: Rare species are expected to be dormant, reducing their own activity/energy requirement, as a survival mechanism, to deal with the current unfavorable growth conditions87.

Thus, species from the same class, like Alphaproteobacteria, Deltaproteobacteria and Thermoprotei in our study, could be predominant in some chimneys but could also be rare in other sites (Supplementary Tables S2–S4 and S7).

tuv.seg: They can be considered “losers” in the competition for nutrients during one certain time period87 and become “winners” at a later time point.
Ils peuvent être considérés comme des « déficitaires » dans la compétition pour les nutriments pendant une certaine période87 et devenir des « bénéficiaires » ultérieurement.

tu.prop: They can be considered “losers” in the competition for nutrients during one certain time period87 and become “winners” at a later time point.
Even if rare species are not active and/or low in abundance in inactive chimneys, mining of these and comparable habitats, i.e. SMS areas, may remove an important genetic reservoir from the deep-sea.

tuv.seg: Thus, species from the same class, like Alphaproteobacteria, Deltaproteobacteria and Thermoprotei in our study, could be predominant in some chimneys but could also be rare in other sites (Supplementary Tables S2–S4 and S7).

Ainsi, des espèces de la même classe, comme les Alphaprotéobactéries, les Deltaprotéobactéries et les Thermoprotéines dans notre étude, pourraient être prédominantes dans certaines cheminées mais pourraient également être rares dans d'autres sites (Tableaux supplémentaires S2 – S4 et S7).

tu.prop: Thus, species from the same class, like Alphaproteobacteria, Deltaproteobacteria and Thermoprotei in our study, could be predominant in some chimneys but could also be rare in other sites (Supplementary Tables S2–S4 and S7).
Our chimneys also appear to host large proportions of bacteria and archaea that are not detectable in any of the other investigated samples.

tuv.seg: Even if rare species are not active and/or low in abundance in inactive chimneys, mining of these and comparable habitats, i.e. SMS areas, may remove an important genetic reservoir from the deep-sea.

Même si les espèces rares ne sont pas actives et/ou sont peu abondantes dans les cheminées inactives, l'exploitation minière de ces habitats et d'habitats comparables, c'est-à-dire les zones SMS, peut éliminer un réservoir génétique important des profondeurs marines.

tu.prop: Even if rare species are not active and/or low in abundance in inactive chimneys, mining of these and comparable habitats, i.e. SMS areas, may remove an important genetic reservoir from the deep-sea.
The proportions of chimney de novo OTUs are much higher than those in most other hydrothermal vents (Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: Our chimneys also appear to host large proportions of bacteria and archaea that are not detectable in any of the other investigated samples.
Nos cheminées semblent également héberger de grandes proportions de bactéries et d’archées qui ne sont détectables dans aucun des autres échantillons étudiés.

tu.prop: Our chimneys also appear to host large proportions of bacteria and archaea that are not detectable in any of the other investigated samples.
The unclassified species consist of “unknown” rare microbial species and other “unknown” organisms which have >0.1% abundance.

tuv.seg: The proportions of chimney de novo OTUs are much higher than those in most other hydrothermal vents (Supplementary Table S1).
Les proportions d'OTU de novo de cheminée sont beaucoup plus élevées que celles de la plupart des autres évents hydrothermaux (tableau supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: The proportions of chimney de novo OTUs are much higher than those in most other hydrothermal vents (Supplementary Table S1).
Because these organisms are currently unculturable and their physiologies remain unknown, it is hard to predict the outcome of their possible disappearance due to deep-sea mining activities.

tuv.seg: The unclassified species consist of “unknown” rare microbial species and other “unknown” organisms which have >0.1% abundance.
Les espèces non classées comprennent des espèces microbiennes rares « inconnues » et d'autres organismes dits « inconnus » dont l'abondance est supérieure à 0,1 %.

tu.prop: The unclassified species consist of “unknown” rare microbial species and other “unknown” organisms which have >0.1% abundance.

tuv.seg: Because these organisms are currently unculturable and their physiologies remain unknown, it is hard to predict the outcome of their possible disappearance due to deep-sea mining activities.

Ces organismes étant actuellement incultivables et leurs physiologies restant inconnues, il est difficile de prédire l’issue de leur éventuelle disparition due aux activités minières en eaux profondes.

tu.prop: Because these organisms are currently unculturable and their physiologies remain unknown, it is hard to predict the outcome of their possible disappearance due to deep-sea mining activities.
The chimney communities, as analogues to SMS environments, appear to largely consist of habitat specific microorganisms including a large number of yet uncharacterized organisms.

tuv.seg: Conclusions
Conclusions

tu.prop: These unknown metabolisms make it the more difficult to predict what the impact will be if these organisms become unavailable for dispersal throughout the open ocean and to foresee the loss for contributing to local and possibly even distant biogeochemical cycling.

tuv.seg: The chimney communities, as analogues to SMS environments, appear to largely consist of habitat specific microorganisms including a large number of yet uncharacterized organisms.

Les communautés de cheminées, analogues aux environnements SMS, semblent être en grande partie constituées de micro-organismes spécifiques à un habitat, y compris d'un grand nombre d'organismes encore non caractérisés.

tu.prop: The chimney communities, as analogues to SMS environments, appear to largely consist of habitat specific microorganisms including a large number of yet uncharacterized organisms.
Also striking is the high number of rare species present in these chimney environments.

tuv.seg: These unknown metabolisms make it the more difficult to predict what the impact will be if these organisms become unavailable for dispersal throughout the open ocean and to foresee the loss for contributing to local and possibly even distant biogeochemical cycling.

Ces métabolismes inconnus rendent d’autant plus difficile la prévision de l’impact si ces organismes ne peuvent plus se disperser dans la zone pélagique et la prévision des pertes dues à leur contribution au cycle biogéochimique local et peut-être même lointaine.

tu.prop: These unknown metabolisms make it the more difficult to predict what the impact will be if these organisms become unavailable for dispersal throughout the open ocean and to foresee the loss for contributing to local and possibly even distant biogeochemical cycling.
These habitats may represent a unique seed library for genetic resources.

tuv.seg: Also striking is the high number of rare species present in these chimney environments.
Il est également frappant de constater le nombre élevé d’espèces rares présentes dans ces environnements de cheminées.

tu.prop: Also striking is the high number of rare species present in these chimney environments.
Removal of rare species in a given habitat may not appear crucial.

tuv.seg: These habitats may represent a unique seed library for genetic resources.
Ces habitats peuvent représenter une bibliothèque de sources uniques pour les ressources génétiques.

tu.prop: These habitats may represent a unique seed library for genetic resources.

However, as many of the rare sequences found in our analyses are very distinct from the known reference sequences, we only have a limited knowledge of the role they may play in other more growth-favorable environments, where they may be responsible for large element turnovers.

tuv.seg: Removal of rare species in a given habitat may not appear crucial.
La suppression d’espèces rares dans un habitat donné peut ne pas sembler cruciale.

tu.prop: Removal of rare species in a given habitat may not appear crucial.
Hence, the removal of SMS habitats through mining activities may not only have an impact on microbial diversity and biogeochemical cycling locally but may also be relevant on a global scale.

tuv.seg: However, as many of the rare sequences found in our analyses are very distinct from the known reference sequences, we only have a limited knowledge of the role they may play in other more growth-favorable environments, where they may be responsible for large element turnovers.

Cependant, comme bon nombre des séquences rares trouvées dans nos analyses sont très distinctes des séquences de référence connues, nous n'avons qu'une connaissance limitée du rôle qu'elles peuvent jouer dans d'autres environnements plus favorables à la croissance, où elles peuvent être responsables d'importants renouvellements d'éléments.

tu.prop: However, as many of the rare sequences found in our analyses are very distinct from the known reference sequences, we only have a limited knowledge of the role they may play in other more growth-favorable environments, where they may be responsible for large element turnovers.

In summary, it seems unjustifiable to remove a precious genetic resource from an unsustainable environment – for resolving current problems related to a high demand for minerals – given the limited understanding we have of the role that these organisms could play in the immediate or even in a distant environment.

tuv.seg: Hence, the removal of SMS habitats through mining activities may not only have an impact on microbial diversity and biogeochemical cycling locally but may also be relevant on a global scale.

Par conséquent, la suppression des habitats SMS par les activités minières peut non seulement avoir un impact sur la diversité microbienne et le cycle biogéochimique au niveau local, mais peut également être pertinente à l’échelle mondiale.

tu.prop: Hence, the removal of SMS habitats through mining activities may not only have an impact on microbial diversity and biogeochemical cycling locally but may also be relevant on a global scale.

tuv.seg: In summary, it seems unjustifiable to remove a precious genetic resource from an unsustainable environment – for resolving current problems related to a high demand for minerals – given the limited understanding we have of the role that these organisms could play in the immediate or even in a distant environment.

En résumé, il semble injustifiable de retirer une précieuse ressource génétique d’un environnement non durable (pour résoudre les problèmes actuels liés à une forte demande en minéraux) étant donné la compréhension limitée que nous avons du rôle que ces organismes jouent dans l’immédiat et dans un avenir lointain.
